short_description: Tasmanian peated single malt with smoke, dark fruit, and warming spice—a full-bodied spirit for contemplative sipping.
description: Lark Tasmanian Peated Single Malt is a full-bodied Australian whisky defined by its distinctive peat character. The spirit opens with prominent smoke and dark fruit notes, supported by vanilla and oak from cask maturation. Secondary layers reveal warming spice and cocoa complexity, while tertiary development introduces leather and subtle earth tones. The medium acidity and tannin structure provide balance and length, making this a contemplative dram suited to evening enjoyment or pairing with rich, smoky cuisine.

name: Glenfiddich 15 Years Old Chinese New Year Gift Pack 2025
short_description: Speyside's Solera innovation: three cask types married in an Oregon pine vat never emptied since 1998, distilled at Dufftown's Robbie Dhu spring.
description: When Glenfiddich introduced the Solera Vat in 1998, it transplanted a sherry-bodega technique into the heart of Speyside — an Oregon pine tun that has never been fully emptied, meaning every bottle of the 15 Year Old carries a molecular trace of that original fill. The distillery sits at the base of the Conval Hills in Dufftown, drawing exclusively from the Robbie Dhu Spring, a mineral-rich highland source whose catchment the Grant family protected by acquiring the surrounding land — the same water used for mashing, fermentation, and reduction to bottling strength. Three distinct cask types — virgin American oak, ex-bourbon, and ex-Oloroso sherry — each contribute for a minimum of 15 years before the whisky enters the Solera Vat, where the perpetual reserve integrates successive fills into a single, layered character: the virgin oak drives coconut and toasted vanilla, ex-bourbon adds ripe orchard fruit, and ex-sherry contributes dried fig and warm spice. Glenfiddich's distinctively small, swan-neck copper pot stills — pear-shaped wash stills (~9,000 L) and constricted-neck spirit stills (~4,500 L) — produce a light, high-ester new make that preserves fruit clarity through decades of wood contact. The three-cask Solera architecture is unmatched in the standard Speyside core range, producing a depth of integration no single-cask-type maturation at this age statement can replicate. Bottled at 40% ABV, chill-filtered, on-site at Dufftown. This 2025 Chinese New Year gift pack presents the expression in commemorative packaging.

name: Glenfiddich 18 Years Old Chinese New Year Gift Pack 2025
short_description: Speyside 18YO from Dufftown's Robbie Dhu spring — 18–20% Oloroso sherry cask component married in oak tuns, bottled on-site at 40% ABV.
description: William Grant broke ground on Glenfiddich in 1886 using secondhand stills and family labour, running first spirit on Christmas Day 1887 — a founding act of stubborn self-sufficiency that persists today: Glenfiddich remains one of very few Speyside distilleries to distil, mature, and bottle entirely on a single site in Dufftown. The 18 Year Old is the expression where that patience becomes most legible. Water drawn exclusively from the Robbie Dhu Spring — a mineral-rich highland source in the Conval Hills, whose catchment the Grant family protected by acquiring the surrounding land — feeds both fermentation and reduction to bottling strength, threading a consistent mineral signature through every stage. The Glen of the River Fiddich's stable valley microclimate and stone-walled dunnage warehouses with earthen floors slow the interaction between spirit and wood, allowing the 18–20% ex-Oloroso sherry European oak component to integrate gradually alongside the dominant ex-bourbon American white oak casks over a minimum of 18 years. Before bottling, all constituent casks are married in large oak tuns — a mandatory house step that rounds the seams between bourbon-driven orchard fruit and sherry-derived dried-fruit depth. What distinguishes the 18 YO within the core range is precisely this sherry proportion: meaningfully higher than the 12 YO's approximate 15%, yet the distillery's characteristically small, swan-neck pot stills preserve the light, high-reflux new make that prevents the sherry from dominating. The 2025 Chinese New Year gift pack presents this age statement in commemorative packaging without altering the liquid.

name: Johnnie Walker  Black Ruby (1 L)
short_description: A ruby-cask-finished expression from Johnnie Walker's pan-Scotland blending house — ~40 malt and grain whiskies, minimum 12 years, 1L.
description: John Walker's founding insight in 1820 Kilmarnock was borrowed directly from tea merchants: blend across regions and seasons to eliminate batch variation — a principle that now governs a reserve of approximately 10 million casks under master blender Dr. Emma Walker, the first woman to hold the role in the brand's two-century history. Black Ruby applies that architecture to a ruby-cask finishing layer over the standard Black Label base, drawing on approximately 40 malt and grain whiskies all aged a minimum of 12 years. The structural spine remains the house trilogy: Cardhu (Speyside) for honeyed fruit-forward sweetness, Caol Ila (Islay) for clean maritime peat, and Clynelish (Highland) for waxy coastal depth — a combination architecturally impossible for any single-region producer to replicate. The ruby-cask finish is the transgression here: introducing dried-fruit richness and deeper color into a blend category that conventionally relies on ex-bourbon and ex-Sherry oak alone. Bottled at 40% ABV and chill-filtered for batch consistency. No vintage declaration applies — the non-vintage philosophy is deliberate, prioritizing house character over year-to-year variation.

name: The Macallan  The Harmony Collection Vibrant Oak (700 ml)
short_description: Easter Elchies Estate Speyside malt shaped by American oak-forward cask selection — Macallan's Harmony series breaks from its sherry-bomb signature.
description: The Harmony Collection is The Macallan's deliberate departure from its own orthodoxy: where the core Sherry Oak range is built on 100% Oloroso-seasoned European oak, Vibrant Oak pivots toward American oak casks, producing a lighter, more citrus-driven profile that sits in direct contrast to the raisin-and-dark-chocolate house signature. That transgression is the point. Distilled at Easter Elchies Estate on the north-west slopes of the River Spey valley near Craigellachie, Moray — 485 acres of alluvial soil fed by on-site spring water — the spirit begins its life in famously squat, 3,900-litre spirit stills cast from death-mask replicas of the original Macallan shapes by coppersmith Forsyths. That small-still geometry limits copper-vapour contact, producing a heavier, oilier new make than any Speyside peer of comparable volume. Double distillation from unpeated barley mashed in a 17-tonne lauter mash tun, fermented ~55–60 hours in 35,000-litre stainless washbacks to ~9.5% ABV wash. The Harmony series is bottled at natural colour — no E150a added — with all hue derived entirely from wood. What distinguishes Vibrant Oak within the Macallan portfolio is precisely this: American oak's faster-extracting vanillin and coconut lactones are allowed to lead, a configuration the distillery almost never permits in its flagship lines.

name: Nikka MIYAGIKYO Discovery - Peated (700 ml)
short_description: Miyagikyo's steam-heated, high-reflux stills meet deliberate peating — a structural inversion of the distillery's signature floral identity.
description:
When Masataka Taketsuru established Miyagikyo in 1969, he engineered its pot stills with bulging necks and upward lyne arms specifically to maximise reflux and strip away heavy congeners — the architectural opposite of his coal-fired Yoichi stills. The Discovery Peated release deliberately contradicts that founding logic, introducing peat smoke into a distillery whose entire still design was built to produce lightness.

Miyagikyo sits in a forested valley west of Sendai, fed by the Hirose and Nikkawa rivers drawing snowmelt from the Zao Mountain Range. That water source is among the softest of any Japanese distillery, and the humid, cool forest microclimate encourages slow, even maturation — conditions that here work to integrate smoke rather than amplify it.

Steam-heated distillation at 130°C drives the high-reflux character even in this peated iteration, meaning the smoke arrives through a lighter, more aromatic spirit base than a direct-fired still would produce. Fermentation runs through 22 stainless-steel washbacks, preserving ester development. Cask regime details are not publicly disclosed by Nikka.

The special feature: peat smoke filtered through a still architecture engineered to suppress heavy compounds — producing a phenolic profile with unusual delicacy rather than the coastal weight of Yoichi.

As a non-vintage expression, character is consistent across batches; the drinking window is open now, with the light spirit base making extended cellaring less critical than for heavier peated malts.

name: Glen Scotia  Festival Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
short_description: Campbeltown single malt with peaty smoke, coastal character, and warming oak spice—a versatile dram for meat and dark chocolate.
description: Glen Scotia is an active single malt Scotch whisky from Campbeltown, Scotland's historic whisky region. This expression balances peaty smoke and coastal brine in the primary layer with vanilla oak sweetness from cask maturation, finishing with leather and tobacco complexity that deepens with time in glass. Medium-full bodied with moderate spice and structure, it suits grilled red meat, braised beef, and cured charcuterie, as well as dark chocolate pairings.

name: Glen Scotia  10 Year Old Unpeated Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
The Legendary Silkie The Midnight Silkie Irish Whiskey (700 ml)
Kilchoman Batch Strength (700 ml)
short_description: Islay single malt with bold peat smoke, coastal brine, and dark malt complexity. Batch-strength intensity.
description: Kilchoman Batch Strength is a non-age-statement single malt from Islay's active distillery, bottled at cask strength to preserve the full character of the spirit. The profile opens with assertive peat smoke and briny coastal minerality, grounded by dark malt and heather-honey sweetness. Oak influence introduces charred wood, vanilla, and black pepper; extended maturation adds layers of dried fruit, leather, and earthy complexity. A full-bodied, warming dram suited to contemplative sipping or pairing with robust cuisine.

name: Glenfiddich Sherry Cask Finish 12 Years Old (700 ml)
short_description: Speyside's lightest house style meets Oloroso sherry: ~15% ex-sherry casks, 6-month tun marriage, Robbie Dhu spring water, bottled at Dufftown.
description:
When William Grant ran the first spirit at Dufftown on Christmas Day 1887, he built his stills small and pear-shaped by design — a deliberate choice to maximise copper contact and strip out heavy congeners, locking in the light, orchard-fruit character that defines Glenfiddich to this day. That same logic governs the 12 Year Old Sherry Cask Finish: the house's signature delicacy is the canvas, and Oloroso sherry oak is the contrast layer, not the foundation.

The distillery sits at the base of the Conval Hills in the Glen of the River Fiddich, drawing exclusively from the Robbie Dhu Spring — a mineral-rich highland source whose catchment the Grant family protected by acquiring the surrounding land. The valley's stable microclimate feeds stone-walled dunnage warehouses with earthen floors, where temperature swings are slow and maturation is unhurried.

The cask regime runs approximately 85% ex-bourbon American white oak / 15% ex-Oloroso sherry European oak for a minimum of 12 years, followed by a mandatory 6-month marriage in 2,000-litre oak tuns — a step that integrates the two oak streams into a single coherent spirit rather than leaving them in tension. Reduction to 40% ABV uses Robbie Dhu spring water; the expression is chill-filtered at standard strength.

What sets this apart within the Speyside 12-year tier is the deliberate restraint of the sherry component: where peers lean into sherry as the dominant signature, Glenfiddich uses it as a structural counterweight to its characteristically lean new make — dried stone fruit and baking spice arriving against a backdrop of green pear and vanilla rather than overwhelming them. No vintage declaration applies; this is a non-vintage, age-statement expression from one of the few Scotch distilleries to distill, mature, and bottle entirely on a single site.

name: The Macallan A Night on Earth The First Light (700 ml)
short_description: Easter Elchies Estate malt shaped by sherry-seasoned oak from forest to filled cask — A Night on Earth captures first-light Japan in a limited artistic release.
description: The A Night on Earth: The First Light release belongs to The Macallan's artist-collaboration series, here evoking the moment dawn breaks over Japan — a narrative anchor that is not merely decorative but frames a deliberate departure from the distillery's age-stated core. Produced at the Easter Elchies Estate on the north-west slopes of the River Spey near Craigellachie, the spirit originates from alluvial soils fed by on-site spring water, with double distillation through 3,900-litre squat copper pot stills — craft-distillery scale despite global output — that deliberately limit copper-vapour contact to yield a heavier, oilier new make capable of absorbing deep sherry-cask character. Maturation follows The Macallan's vertically integrated cask programme: sherry-seasoned European and American oak casks, sourced from Galician forests, air-dried, coopered, and Oloroso-seasoned in Jerez before filling — a wood investment unmatched among Speyside peers. Natural colour only, with no added E150a caramel. What sets this expression apart within the wider Macallan portfolio is its position as a no-age-statement limited release built around a single cultural concept rather than a maturation milestone, breaking the distillery's own convention of anchoring prestige to declared age. The result is a sherry-driven, full-bodied Speyside malt carrying dried fruit, ginger, and warm oak spice — a format that trades the chronological hierarchy of the core range for artistic occasion.

name: The Macallan Harmony Collection Phoenix Honey Orchid Tea (700 ml)
short_description: Easter Elchies Estate malt finished with Phoenix Honey Orchid tea — Macallan's sherry-oak architecture meets rare Taiwanese botanical infusion.
description: The Harmony Collection is The Macallan's deliberate departure from its own cask-first orthodoxy: each release introduces a botanical co-author alongside the sherry-seasoned oak that has defined Easter Elchies since Alexander Reid founded the distillery in 1824. The Phoenix Honey Orchid Tea edition draws its secondary character from Mi Lan Xiang (Phoenix Honey Orchid) Dan Cong oolong, a high-mountain cultivar from Guangdong's Fenghuang range, whose floral-honey aromatic compounds are woven into the whisky through a proprietary tea-infusion finishing process — a technique with no precedent in the Speyside canon. The spirit originates on the Easter Elchies Estate, 485 acres on the north-west slopes of the River Spey valley near Craigellachie, where alluvial soils and on-site spring water feed a 17-tonne lauter mash tun and 24 famously squat spirit stills of just 3,900 litres each — craft-distillery scale — producing the heavy, oily new make that is the structural prerequisite for sherry-cask maturation. Maturation proceeds in sherry-seasoned European and American oak casks, sourced through Macallan's vertically integrated wood programme: staves air-dried, coopered, and Oloroso-seasoned in Jerez before filling. Natural colour only; no added caramel (E150a). The special feature is categorical: no other Speyside distillery has formally co-authored a whisky with a named single-cultivar oolong at this price tier. Drinking window: open now; the tea-derived florals are most expressive within 2–3 years of release.

name: The GlenAllachie Oloroso / Sherry Cask Finish (700 ml)
short_description: Speyside single malt finished in Oloroso Sherry casks; non-chill filtered, 46%+ ABV, from Billy Walker's radically downsized GlenAllachie.
description:
For its first five decades, GlenAllachie operated as an anonymous blending workhorse for Chivas Brothers — its spirit dissolved into Clan Campbell and Chivas Regal with no single malt identity of its own. When Billy Walker acquired the distillery in 2017, he slashed output by roughly 87% and deployed one of Speyside's most hands-on cask programmes, with over 30% of distillate maturing in premium ex-Sherry wood — the foundation this Oloroso finish draws directly from.

The distillery sits at the foot of Ben Rinnes near Aberlour, Banffshire, drawing process water from Blackstank and Heanshead Dams fed by spring- and snowmelt streams on the mountain's north-east slopes. That cold, mineral-rich water supports a 160–164 hour fermentation in 60,000-litre stainless steel washbacks — among Scotland's longest standard fermentation windows — engineered specifically to maximise ester formation and build the muscular, fruit-dense new make that Oloroso wood can amplify rather than overwhelm.

The spirit is double-distilled through lantern-shaped wash stills and onion-shaped spirit stills fitted with horizontal shell-and-tube condensers (an energy-efficient gravity-fed layout unique to the site), then finished in first- and second-fill Oloroso Sherry casks under Walker's strict no-third-fill policy. Bottled at a minimum 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, natural colour — no cosmetic intervention.

What sets this expression apart is the deliberate pairing of an unusually long fermentation-derived ester load with Oloroso's oxidative, walnut-and-dried-fruit character, producing a density rarely achieved at this price point in Speyside.

name: The GlenAllachie Cask Strength (700 ml)
short_description: Cask-strength Speyside from the foot of Ben Rinnes — 160-hour fermentation, first/second-fill Sherry puncheons, non-chill filtered, natural colour.
description:
For its first five decades, GlenAllachie was an anonymous blending workhorse for Chivas Regal and Clan Campbell — its single malt identity essentially nonexistent. When Billy Walker acquired the distillery in 2017, he slashed output by roughly 87% and extended fermentation to 160–164 hours, among the longest standard fermentation windows in Scotland, a deliberate engineering choice to maximise ester formation and drive the muscular, fruit-forward new make that defines the house today.

The distillery sits at the foot of Ben Rinnes near Aberlour, Banffshire, drawing process water from two damheads — Blackstank and Heanshead — fed by spring- and snowmelt streams off the mountain's north-east slopes. That cold, mineral-clean water feeds a production line built around first- and second-fill casks exclusively, with over 30% of stock maturing in Pedro Ximénez and Oloroso Sherry puncheons and hogsheads alongside ex-Bourbon American oak foundations.

The Cask Strength expression is bottled at natural cask strength without dilution, non-chill filtered, natural colour — no adjustment, no cosmetic intervention. What makes this release genuinely unusual within Speyside is the cask diversity underpinning it: Port, Marsala, Barolo, Madeira, and Moscatel casks all rotate through the programme, giving Walker the blending palette of a wine négociant rather than a conventional malt distillery. Drinking window: now through 10+ years for those who prefer the fruit to integrate further.

name: The GlenAllachie 12 Years old (700 ml)
short_description: Speyside built at the foot of Ben Rinnes — 160-hr fermentation, first/second-fill Sherry puncheons, bottled 46% non-chill filtered.
description: For its first five decades, GlenAllachie was an anonymous blending workhorse for Chivas Brothers — virtually no whisky left the Aberlour site with its own name on the label. When Billy Walker acquired the distillery in 2017, he slashed output by roughly 87% and extended fermentation to 160–164 hours, among the longest standard fermentation windows in Scotland, deliberately maximising ester formation in the new make. The distillery sits at the foot of Ben Rinnes near Aberlour, Banffshire, drawing process water from two spring- and snowmelt-fed damheads on the mountain's north-east slopes — cold, mineral-rich water that supports a slow, clean fermentation. Distillation runs through lantern-shaped wash stills (36,300 L) and onion-shaped spirit stills (23,900 L) fitted with horizontal shell-and-tube condensers — a gravity-fed layout unique to the site — before the spirit enters a cask regime where over 30% of stock matures in first- and second-fill Pedro Ximénez and Oloroso Sherry puncheons and hogsheads, complemented by ex-Bourbon American oak forming the structural backbone. Every expression is bottled at a minimum 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, natural colour. The 12 Year Old draws on pre-2017 inherited stocks — the last generation of spirit before Walker's fermentation and cask overhaul — making it a transitional document between GlenAllachie's blending-house past and its current independent identity. Drink now through 2032.

name: The GlenAllachie 15 Years old (700 ml)
short_description: Speyside built at the foot of Ben Rinnes — 160-hr fermentation, first/second-fill Sherry puncheons, non-chill filtered at 46% ABV.
description: When Billy Walker acquired GlenAllachie in 2017, his first act was to slash output by roughly 87% and extend fermentation to 160–164 hours — among the longest standard runs in Scotland — deliberately maximising ester formation in the new make before a single cask was filled. The distillery sits at the foot of Ben Rinnes near Aberlour, Banffshire, drawing process water from Blackstank and Heanshead Dams fed by spring- and snowmelt-fed streams on the mountain's north-east slopes; that cold, mineral-rich water feeds horizontal shell-and-tube condensers — an architectural necessity of the gravity-fed site — producing a heavier, oilier spirit cut than vertical condensers typically yield. The 15 Year Old draws on a cask regime of first- and second-fill only, with the foundation in ex-Bourbon American oak and over 30% of the blend from Pedro Ximénez and Oloroso Sherry puncheons and hogsheads, supplemented by wine-cask finishes across Port, Marsala, Madeira, and Barolo. Bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, natural colour — no caramel, no cold-stripping of texture. The special feature: this expression draws on pre-2017 inherited stocks, meaning the cask selection reflects Walker's editorial eye applied to spirit he did not distil — a curatorial act rather than a production one. Dried dark fruit, baking spice, and a honeyed mid-palate carry through a long, warming finish; drink now through the mid-2030s.

name: The GlenAllachie 2011 Oloroso Hogshead Cask no. 1950 (700 ml)
short_description: Single Oloroso hogshead from GlenAllachie's 2011 vintage — cask no. 1950, bottled at natural strength, non-chill filtered, from Speyside's most cask-obsessive independent.
description:
When Billy Walker's consortium acquired GlenAllachie in 2017, one of the first acts was to audit the inherited warehouses — and it was among those pre-2017 casks that single barrels like Cask no. 1950 were identified. Distilled in 2011 under the Chivas Brothers era, this spirit entered a single Oloroso hogshead and matured undisturbed in one of the distillery's 16 on-site warehouses beside the Lour burn, at the foot of Ben Rinnes in Aberlour, Banffshire. The site's spring- and snowmelt-fed water and cool Speyside microclimate slow oxidation through the warehouse walls, allowing the hogshead's tighter wood grain to transfer Oloroso character incrementally rather than aggressively.

The production signature here is GlenAllachie's 160–164 hour fermentation — among the longest standard fermentation windows in Scotland — which builds a dense ester load in the new make before a single drop of sherry wood enters the equation. Double distillation through lantern-shaped wash stills (36,300 L) and onion-shaped spirit stills (23,900 L), combined with horizontal shell-and-tube condensers, preserves that fruity, muscular character into the cask. Bottled at natural cask strength, non-chill filtered, natural colour — no dilution, no fining.

The special feature: a hogshead (roughly 250 L) concentrates Oloroso influence more intensely than GlenAllachie's more common Oloroso puncheons, compressing the interaction between spirit and wood into a tighter, more assertive exchange. This is a pre-Walker distillate shaped entirely by post-Walker curation philosophy.

The profile opens with vanilla and oak character from cask maturation, supported by warm spice and hazelnut notes. Dark fruit and cocoa emerge in the mid-palate, with a gentle smoke undertone adding complexity. Medium-full body and moderate acidity create a smooth, rounded mouthfeel suitable for neat enjoyment or as a foundation for premium cocktails.

name: De Bortoli  Noble One Botrytis (375 ml)
short_description: Luscious botrytis Semillon with honeyed stone fruit, citrus brightness, and elegant complexity in a half-bottle format.
description: De Bortoli's Noble One represents the pinnacle of Australian botrytis-affected Semillon production. This 375 ml dessert wine showcases the distinctive character of noble rot: honeyed apricot and stone fruit on the nose, with layered floral and citrus zest notes that evolve on the palate. The medium body and waxy texture are balanced by crisp medium-high acidity, preventing cloying sweetness and extending the finish. Ideal for sipping as an aperitif, pairing with fruit-based desserts, or enjoying alongside blue cheese.

name: Taylor’s  Special Ruby Port
short_description: Smooth, full-bodied Ruby Port with dark fruit, chocolate, and warming spice—a classic fortified digestif.
description: Taylor's Special Ruby Port is a non-vintage fortified wine blending Touriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, and Tinta Roriz—the classic trio of Portuguese Port varieties. This full-bodied expression delivers ripe dark plum and berry on the nose, with supporting notes of chocolate, vanilla oak, and warming spice. The palate is smooth and approachable, with a lingering finish that makes it ideal as a digestif or paired with dark chocolate and rich desserts.

Craggy Range  Te Muna Pinot Noir Martinborough
short_description: Elegant Martinborough Pinot Noir with red cherry, strawberry, and subtle oak—silky, food-friendly, and age-worthy.
description: Craggy Range's Te Muna Pinot Noir from Martinborough showcases the region's signature elegance and restraint. The 2021 vintage opens with bright red cherry and strawberry, underpinned by a whisper of spice and oak integration. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure deliver a silky mouthfeel, while subtle earth and leather notes emerge on the finish, suggesting complexity that will develop with bottle age. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, and lighter grilled preparations.

name: Banrock Station  Shiraz
short_description: Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with ripe blackberry, pepper spice, and smoky depth—a food-friendly classic.
description: Banrock Station Shiraz from Australia's Riverland region delivers a full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackberry and dark plum fruit. Black pepper and spice dominate the mid-palate, supported by subtle smoke and eucalyptus notes characteristic of warm-climate Syrah. The wine's medium-high tannins and leather undertones provide structure and aging potential, making it equally suited to immediate enjoyment or short-term cellaring. A versatile, approachable expression of Australian Shiraz.

name: Carpineto  Dogajolo Sangiovese & Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese and Cabernet: ripe red fruit, cedar spice, and earthy complexity.
description: Carpineto's Dogajolo represents a classic Tuscan approach to blending indigenous Sangiovese with Cabernet Sauvignon, creating a wine of medium-full body and balanced structure. The primary expression centers on ripe red cherry and plum, supported by herbaceous notes characteristic of Sangiovese. Secondary oak and cedar notes add complexity, while subtle earth and tobacco undertones emerge on the finish, suggesting careful aging and integration. Food-friendly and versatile, this blend suits grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Catena Zapata  Alamos Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Mendoza Cabernet with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannin—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description: Catena Zapata's Alamos Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza delivers a classic expression of the varietal in Argentina's premier wine region. Dark fruit—blackcurrant and plum—dominates the nose and palate, supported by herbaceous notes and a hint of cedar. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with subtle vanilla and earth notes adding complexity. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and hearty fare.

name: Catena Zapata  Alamos Malbec
short_description: Ripe Mendoza Malbec with dark plum, blackberry, and spice—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and braises.
description: Catena Zapata's Alamos Malbec is a single-varietal expression from Mendoza, Argentina's premier wine region. The wine displays the hallmark character of Malbec: ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit with supporting notes of spice and vanilla oak. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes. The subtle earth and leather notes that emerge on the finish add complexity and food-pairing flexibility, extending appeal to cured meats and comfort-food preparations.

name: Bodegas Palacios Remondo  Camins del Priorat
short_description: Priorat blend of Garnacha, Cariena & Cabernet—dark fruit, mineral edge, food-friendly structure.
description: Bodegas Palacios Remondo's Camins del Priorat is a Garnacha-led blend (60% Cariena, 10% Cabernet Sauvignon & Syrah) from Spain's renowned Priorat region. Dark cherry and blackberry dominate the nose, layered with slate minerality and black pepper spice. The palate shows medium-full body with medium-high tannins, delivering a structured, food-friendly profile. Earthy undertones and licorice notes add complexity. Ideal for grilled red meat, lamb, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Langmeil  Three Gardens, Shiraz-Mourvedre - Grenache
short_description: Full-bodied Barossa GSM blend with ripe blackberry, plum, and spice—a structured wine for red meat and game.
description: Langmeil's Three Gardens is a Rhône-inspired blend of Shiraz, Mourvèdre, and Grenache from Barossa Valley, delivering a full-bodied expression of dark fruit and savory spice. Blackberry and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla and cocoa from oak aging, while smoke and black pepper add tertiary complexity. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide structure and aging potential, making this wine an excellent match for grilled red meat, braised beef, and game preparations.

name: Catena Zapata  Catena Alta Melbec
short_description: Ripe Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit, vanilla oak, and structured tannins—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Catena Zapata's Catena Alta Malbec is a benchmark expression of Mendoza's signature varietal, showcasing the region's ability to produce dark-fruited, structured reds. The 2022 vintage delivers dominant blackberry and dark plum on the nose, with supporting notes of vanilla and oak from careful winemaking. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide backbone without heaviness, making this wine approachable now yet capable of modest cellaring. The finish carries subtle tobacco and earth, adding complexity to an otherwise fruit-forward profile.

name: Fantinel  Sant' Helena Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Friuli Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and balanced tannin—food-friendly Italian red.
description: Fantinel's Sant' Helena Cabernet Sauvignon from Friuli-Venezia Giulia delivers the classic varietal profile expected from northeastern Italy's premier red-wine region. Ripe blackcurrant and black cherry dominate the nose, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes that reflect the wine's cool-climate terroir. Medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make this wine approachable in youth while offering enough grip for food pairing versatility. The finish carries hints of black pepper and earth, suggesting modest aging potential.

name: Vigneti Di Zabu  IL Passo Nerello Mascalese Terre Siciiane IGT
short_description: Sicilian Nerello Mascalese with red cherry, volcanic minerality, and elegant spice—food-friendly and age-worthy.
description: Vigneti Di Zabu's IL Passo Nerello Mascalese is a single-varietal expression from Sicily's volcanic terroir. The wine opens with dominant red fruit and cherry character, supported by earthy minerality and black pepper spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it versatile at table, equally at home with grilled lamb, Mediterranean herb-driven dishes, or mushroom-based preparations. Subtle tobacco and mineral complexity suggest modest aging potential.

name: Errazuriz  Estate Reserva Carmenere
short_description: Carmenère from Chile's Aconcagua Valley: dark fruit, spice, and herbaceous character in a food-friendly, medium-full frame.
description: Errazuriz Estate Reserva Carmenère is a single-varietal red wine from Chile's Aconcagua Valley, showcasing the signature character of Carmenère—a grape that thrives in the region's warm, well-drained terroir. The wine displays dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit on the nose and palate, complemented by herbaceous bell pepper notes and subtle spice. Secondary oak aging introduces cocoa and vanilla undertones, while tertiary development brings earthy, leather-tinged complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at the table, pairing naturally with grilled red meats, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: Sileni Estates  Cellar Selection Pinot Noir Hawke's Bay
short_description: Elegant Hawke's Bay Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile table wine for poultry and lighter fare.
description: Sileni Estates' Cellar Selection Pinot Noir from Hawke's Bay showcases the region's signature elegance and restraint. The wine opens with vibrant red fruit and raspberry notes, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of stone fruit. On the palate, silky tannins and bright acidity create a refined, food-friendly profile that avoids heaviness while maintaining complexity. The fresh, summery character makes this an ideal companion for roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, and lighter seafood preparations.

name: Torres  Celeste Ribera Del Duero
short_description: Elegant Ribera del Duero Tempranillo with ripe dark fruit, smooth tannins, and balanced structure—food-friendly and approachable.
description:
Torres Celeste Ribiera del Duero is a single-varietal Tempranillo (Tinto Fino) from Spain's prestigious Ribiera del Duero region. This wine showcases the hallmark character of the appellation: deep, ripe dark fruit—blackcurrant and black cherry—with a smooth, well-integrated palate and medium-full body. Oak aging contributes subtle spice and vanilla notes without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile, making this wine both elegant and approachable for everyday enjoyment or food pairing.

The wine's balanced acidity and medium tannin structure provide excellent versatility at the table, from grilled meats and lamb to tomato-based pasta and roasted poultry. A refined expression of Tempranillo that rewards both immediate drinking and modest cellaring.

name: Wolf Blass  Yellow Label Shiraz
short_description: Full-bodied Barossa Shiraz with dark fruit, spice, and oak—a fine-dining staple.
description: Wolf Blass Yellow Label Shiraz from Barossa Valley delivers a full-bodied expression of Australia's signature varietal. Blackberry and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla and cocoa notes from oak aging. Black pepper and clove spice add complexity, while a subtle smoke character rounds the finish. High tannins and medium acidity provide structure suitable for premium red-meat cuisine and aged cheese pairings.

name: Wolf Blass  Yellow Label Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Barossa Valley Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, vanilla oak, and peppery spice—a food-friendly classic.
description: Wolf Blass Yellow Label Cabernet Sauvignon from Barossa Valley delivers a classic Australian red wine profile. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla and oak from winemaking, with subtle smoke and black pepper adding complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured enough for hearty dishes. A reliable, well-crafted expression suited to fine dining and everyday entertaining alike.

name: Wolf Blass  Gold Label Barossa Shiraz
short_description: Full-bodied Barossa Shiraz with dark fruit, spice, and oak complexity—a fine-dining staple.
description: Wolf Blass Gold Label Barossa Shiraz is a full-bodied red wine showcasing the richness and power characteristic of the Barossa Valley. Dark fruit—blackberry and plum—dominates the palate, supported by layers of black pepper, clove, and vanilla oak. Cocoa and smoke notes add depth and complexity, making this wine a versatile choice for premium dining occasions and substantial meat dishes.

name: Wolf Blass  Bilyara Shiraz
short_description: Ripe Barossa Shiraz with blackberry, plum, and oak—a food-friendly, medium-full red for fine dining and everyday occasions.
description: Wolf Blass Bilyara Shiraz is a classic Barossa Valley expression showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit character. Blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by well-integrated oak, vanilla, and black pepper from winemaking. Subtle chocolate and tobacco notes add depth and complexity. Medium-full body with balanced acidity and moderate tannin make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or roasted poultry. A reliable, approachable choice for fine dining and everyday enjoyment.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Entry-level Barossa Cabernet from Australia's oldest family winery — vegan since 2012, built on the same wild-ferment philosophy as the ฿65k tier.
description:
Samuel Smith planted the first vines at Angaston in 1849, and six generations later the Hill-Smith family still controls every decision — including the choice to make every wine in the portfolio, from this Y Series to The Caley, 100% vegan since the 2012 vintage, eliminating all animal-derived fining agents across the range. That consistency of principle is the Y Series' quiet credential.

The fruit draws on Barossa Valley floor sites — warm, low-elevation, with the deep alluvial soils that push Cabernet Sauvignon toward plush dark-fruit concentration rather than the leaner, more angular profile of cooler-climate Australian Cabernet. The heat retention of the valley floor accelerates phenolic ripeness, producing soft, approachable tannin structure.

Winemaking follows the house's minimal-intervention philosophy: open-top fermentation, with the same wild/spontaneous fermentation approach used to initiate premium-tier reds. Oak treatment is restrained at this tier, preserving primary fruit character. No animal-derived fining agents are used at any production level.

What separates this from category peers at the price point is institutional lineage: the Y Series is made under the same roof — and the same cooperage — as Octavius and The Caley, giving it access to winemaking infrastructure that no other sub-฿1,000 Australian Cabernet can claim.

Drink on release through 3–4 years for primary fruit freshness.

name: Catena Zapata  Catena Malbec
short_description: Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit intensity, cocoa spice, and leather complexity—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description: Catena Zapata's Catena Malbec is a single-varietal expression from Mendoza, Argentina's premier Malbec region. The wine displays a medium-full body with dark plum and blackberry on the nose, supported by cocoa and spice from careful winemaking. On the palate, medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while tertiary leather and tobacco notes add savory complexity. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: Matua  Pinot Noir
short_description: Marlborough Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description: Matua's Marlborough Pinot Noir delivers the region's signature bright red-fruit character with a medium-full body and refined tannin structure. Aromas of cherry and strawberry lead to a palate marked by plum and subtle oak influence, finishing with earthy minerality and balanced acidity. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, mushroom dishes, and lighter red-meat preparations.

name: Michel Lynch  Nature Merlot Bordeaux "Organic" AOC
short_description: Organic Merlot from Bordeaux with ripe plum, cherry, and soft tannins—food-friendly and approachable.
description: Michel Lynch Nature Merlot is a certified organic expression from Bordeaux, crafted from 100% Merlot. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe red and dark fruit character—black cherry and plum dominate the palate—supported by soft, integrated tannins and a subtle earthy undertone. Balanced acidity and approachable structure make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or herb-roasted poultry.

name: Cloudy Bay  Pinot Noir
short_description: Marlborough Pinot Noir with vibrant red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Cloudy Bay's 2022 Pinot Noir from Marlborough showcases the region's signature elegance and restraint. Bright red fruit and raspberry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle stone fruit and earthy undertones that add complexity without heaviness. The silky tannin structure and crisp acidity create a wine that feels both refined and approachable, making it equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or lighter seafood preparations. A versatile expression of New Zealand Pinot Noir craftsmanship.

name: Terrazas  Reserva Malbec
short_description: Mendoza Malbec with ripe dark fruit, vanilla oak, and food-friendly structure—a versatile red for grilled meats and hearty fare.
description: Terrazas Reserva Malbec from Mendoza showcases the varietal's signature dark fruit character—ripe plum and blackberry—with supporting vanilla and spice notes from oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet complex enough for cellaring. A classic Argentine expression suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and Mediterranean-style dishes.

name: Terrazas  Grand Malbec
short_description: Ripe Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit, vanilla oak, and smooth tannins—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description: Terrazas Grand Malbec is a single-varietal expression from Mendoza, Argentina's premier wine region. The 2021 vintage showcases the signature character of Malbec: generous dark plum and blackberry fruit on the nose, supported by subtle vanilla and spice from oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured, with an earthy undertone that emerges on the finish. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and hearty fare.

name: Echeverria  Founder's Selection
short_description: Maipo Valley Cabernet-led blend with dark fruit, spice, and structured tannin—food-friendly Chilean classic.
description: Echeverria's Founder's Selection is a Maipo Valley red blend anchored by 85% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Syrah and Carmenere. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, layered with black pepper spice and subtle cedar. Mid-palate tannins are structured yet approachable, with leather and tobacco notes emerging on the finish. A versatile, well-balanced expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Solar Viejo  Crianza
short_description: Bold Rioja Tempranillo with dark plum, balanced tannin, and food-friendly structure. Crianza aging adds subtle oak complexity.
description: Solar Viejo Crianza is a classically styled Rioja Tempranillo that delivers approachable depth and versatility. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and red fruit aromatics, supported by subtle oak and black pepper notes from its Crianza aging. Medium-full body and medium tannin provide structure without heaviness, while medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food compatibility. Earthy undertones emerge on the finish, adding complexity to an otherwise fruit-forward profile. A reliable choice for everyday red wine enjoyment and table pairing.

name: Coastal Ridge  Merlot
short_description: Approachable California Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for weeknight dinners.
description: Coastal Ridge Merlot is a medium-full-bodied California red showcasing the varietal's signature plum and berry-forward character. Ripe red fruit and cranberry notes dominate the palate, supported by subtle vanilla undertones and integrated tannins that deliver a smooth, juicy finish. The herbaceous hints add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-driven profile, making this wine an accessible choice for casual dining and food-friendly occasions.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley Merlot
short_description: Napa Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Duckhorn's 2022 Napa Valley Merlot delivers a classic expression of the varietal: generous plum and berry fruit on the nose, with hints of vanilla oak and subtle herbaceous notes. The palate is juicy and well-balanced, with integrated tannins and a smooth, approachable texture that makes this wine versatile at the table. Medium-Full body and moderate acidity create a wine suited to both casual entertaining and paired dinners.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, vanilla oak, and structured tannins—age-worthy and elegant.
description: Duckhorn's 2022 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon exemplifies the region's signature style: a full-bodied red wine built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit. Vanilla oak and cedar notes from careful barrel aging provide secondary complexity, while emerging tobacco and earth undertones hint at the wine's cellaring potential. High tannin structure and medium-high acidity frame the palate with authority, making this wine an excellent match for grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes. The wine's balance of fruit-forward appeal and structural sophistication positions it as a versatile table wine for both immediate enjoyment and medium-term aging.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley 'Three Palms Vineyard' Merlot
short_description: Elegant Napa Merlot with ripe plum, cherry, and integrated oak; soft tannins and balanced structure.
description: Duckhorn's Three Palms Vineyard Merlot from Napa Valley's 2019 vintage showcases the varietal's signature richness and approachability. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the nose, supported by subtle vanilla oak and herbaceous undertones. The palate delivers a juicy berry medley with well-integrated tannins and a medium-full body that feels polished and refined. Soft texture and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled beef, roasted poultry, or aged cheeses.

name: Francis Coppola  Director's Cut Pinot Noir
McManis  Zinfandel
short_description: California Zinfandel with ripe dark berries, black cherry, and subtle spice—food-friendly and approachable.
description: McManis Zinfandel is a medium-full bodied California red showcasing the varietal's signature ripe dark fruit character. Dominant notes of black cherry and blackberry jam are complemented by subtle pepper and spice, with soft, approachable tannins that make this wine versatile at the table. A straightforward, well-balanced expression suited to grilled meats, BBQ, and casual entertaining.

name: Dominus Estate 2016
short_description: Napanook's dry-farmed volcanic gravel, Moueix's Pomerol discipline: the 2016 is graphite-spined, forest-berried, built for the cellar.
description:
When Christian Moueix — steward of Château Pétrus — planted his philosophy on Napa soil in 1983, he made one decision that set Dominus permanently apart: no irrigation, ever. On Napanook Vineyard's upper blocks, where volcanic gravelly clay-loam drains sharply against the Mayacamas foothills, dry-farming forces root systems deep into alluvial subsoil, producing small berries of dense phenolic concentration that no drip line could replicate.

The 2016 growing season was cool and even — ideal conditions for Napanook's uppermost gravel blocks, where afternoon breezes off San Pablo Bay extended hang time and preserved acidity. Fruit was hand-sorted at harvest and passed through an optical berry-sorter before block-by-block fermentation with gentle pump-overs and a low-pressure vertical press. The wine aged in 100% French oak barrique for approximately 15–18 months, with new oak held to roughly one-third, preserving the site's graphite and iron signature over any wood-derived sweetness.

The special feature here is structural: in a valley that built its reputation on extraction and high new-oak percentages, the 2016 Dominus reads closer to a Pauillac than a Napa Cabernet — cedar, forest berry, and ferrous tension over opulence. Firm tannins demand patience; the drinking window opens around 2026 and extends well beyond.

name: Altos Las Hormigas  Reserve Malbec
short_description: Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit depth, cocoa spice, and structured tannin—a food-friendly Reserve for grilled meats.
description: Altos Las Hormigas Reserve Malbec 2020 is a single-varietal expression from Mendoza's high-altitude vineyards, showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and mineral backbone. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the primary profile, supported by cocoa and warm spice from careful oak aging. Leather and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and age-worthiness. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity make this wine a natural partner for grilled red meat, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes.

name: Glaetzer  Amon-Ra
short_description: Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and oak—a medium-full, food-friendly red for fine dining.
description: Glaetzer's Amon-Ra is a single-varietal Shiraz from Australia's Barossa Valley, showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and structural balance. The wine opens with dominant blackberry and plum aromatics, supported by oak and vanilla from winemaking, with subtle smoke and cocoa complexity. Medium-full body and moderate tannin make it approachable yet age-worthy, ideal for grilled red meats, lamb, and braised dishes. A versatile expression suited to fine-dining service and cellar-friendly collection.

name: Glaetzer  Wallace
short_description: Barossa Shiraz-Grenache blend: dark fruit, spice, and smoke. Food-friendly medium-full red for fine dining.
description: Glaetzer's Wallace is a Barossa Valley Shiraz-Grenache blend (74/26) delivering ripe blackberry and plum fruit with supporting vanilla oak and black pepper spice. Medium-full bodied with balanced acidity and moderate tannin, the wine shows smoke and chocolate complexity on the finish. A versatile, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and smoked preparations.

name: Glaetzer  Anaperenna
short_description: Barossa Valley Shiraz-Cabernet blend with ripe dark fruit, oak spice, and food-friendly structure.
description: Glaetzer's Anaperenna is a Barossa Valley red blend anchored by 75% Shiraz and 25% Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering a medium-full bodied wine with approachable tannin and balanced acidity. The palate opens with ripe blackberry and plum, supported by vanilla oak and subtle smoke, finishing with black pepper and chocolate notes. A versatile, well-crafted expression suited to fine dining and everyday entertaining alike.

name: Torbreck  The Steading
short_description: Grenache-led GSM blend from Barossa Valley with ripe dark fruit, smoke, and structured tannin—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Torbreck's The Steading is a Grenache-dominant blend (60% Grenache, 20% Shiraz, 20% Mataro) from Australia's Barossa Valley, representing the classic Rhône South style adapted to warm continental terroir. The 2022 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with medium tannin and balanced acidity, anchored by dominant blackberry and plum fruit on the nose and palate. Secondary oak, vanilla, smoke, and black pepper notes add textural complexity and spice, while emerging tertiary chocolate and tobacco hints suggest age-worthiness. A versatile, structured red suited to fine dining and serious food pairing.

name: Torbreck  The Factor 2021
short_description: Full-bodied Barossa Shiraz with ripe dark fruit, oak spice, and smoke—a fine-dining red for premium red meat.
description: Torbreck's The Factor is a full-bodied Shiraz from Barossa Valley's premier terroir, showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and structural power. The wine opens with dominant blackberry and plum, supported by secondary oak, vanilla, and black pepper notes that reflect careful winemaking. A subtle smoke character and hints of chocolate add complexity and age-worthiness. Medium acidity and medium-high tannins provide structure without harshness, making this a sophisticated choice for fine dining and premium red-meat cuisine.

name: Torbreck  Run Rig
short_description: Barossa Shiraz-Viognier blend with ripe dark fruit, oak spice, and smoke—a food-friendly, medium-full red.
description: Torbreck's Run Rig is a Barossa Valley Shiraz-dominant blend (98% Shiraz, 2% Viognier) that delivers a classic Australian red wine profile. The wine opens with dominant blackberry and plum fruit, supported by vanilla oak and subtle smoke from winemaking. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it approachable yet age-worthy, with enough complexity to reward contemplation at the table. The Viognier co-ferment adds textural softness and aromatic lift. Ideal for fine dining occasions or as a versatile food companion.

name: Montgras  Antu Ninquen Syrah
short_description: Chilean Syrah with dark plum, blackberry, and peppery spice—medium-full bodied and food-friendly.
description: Montgras Antu Ninquen Syrah is a single-varietal expression from Chile's Colchagua Valley, delivering a medium-full bodied red wine with approachable structure and layered complexity. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the palate, supported by black pepper spice and subtle smoke, with emerging leather and earth notes adding depth. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or hearty preparations.

name: Montgras  Ninquen
short_description: Colchagua Cabernet-Syrah blend with dark fruit, pepper spice, and structured tannins—food-friendly Chilean red.
description: Montgras Ninquen is a Colchagua Valley blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (50%) and Syrah (45%), with 5% Petit Verdot for structure. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe, integrated tannins and medium acidity, delivering dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, layered with black pepper and cedar. Secondary notes of spice and subtle oak support the fruit, while tertiary leather and tobacco complexity emerge with air. A food-friendly Chilean red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-forward dishes.

name: Ata Rangi  Pinot Noir
short_description: Elegant Martinborough Pinot Noir with red fruit purity, earthy complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description: Ata Rangi's 2021 Pinot Noir from Martinborough showcases the region's signature elegance and restraint. Red cherry and strawberry dominate the nose, supported by subtle plum and mineral notes that evolve on the palate. Medium-full body and silky tannins create a refined mouthfeel, while earthy undertones and gentle spice add complexity without heaviness. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, mushroom dishes, and lighter red-meat preparations.

name: Fromm Winery  Pinot Noir La Strada
short_description: Marlborough Pinot Noir with silky tannins, bright red fruit, and elegant structure—versatile food companion.
description: Fromm Winery's La Strada Pinot Noir from Marlborough showcases the region's signature elegance and restraint. Medium-full bodied with medium tannins and bright acidity, this wine balances ripe red cherry and strawberry on the palate with subtle oak integration and earthy undertones. The silky texture and food-friendly profile make it an ideal match for roasted poultry, duck, mushroom risotto, and tomato-based pasta dishes.

name: Delheim Cabernet Sauvignon / Shiraz
short_description: Stellenbosch Cabernet-Shiraz blend: dark fruit, spice, and structured tannin. Food-friendly and age-worthy.
description: A well-balanced Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz blend from Stellenbosch, South Africa. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by black pepper spice and subtle cedar oak. The wine shows medium-full body with integrated tannin structure, offering both immediate appeal and cellaring potential. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with time, adding complexity and savory depth. Versatile with grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb preparations.

name: Lion's Cape  Coastal Red Cabernet Sauvignon (Fruit wine blend)
description: Lion's Cape  Coastal Red Cabernet Sauvignon (Fruit wine blend)

name: Montecillo  Grand Reserva
short_description: Montecillo Grand Reserva 2015: A structured Rioja Tempranillo with dark plum, leather, and vanilla oak—elegant complexity at a premium price point.
description: Montecillo's Grand Reserva 2015 is a classically styled Rioja Tempranillo that showcases the depth and aging potential of Spain's most celebrated red wine region. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and red cherry aromatics, supported by subtle oak-derived vanilla and leather notes that speak to careful barrel aging. On the palate, medium-full body and medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, while medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food-friendliness. Tertiary tobacco and cedar notes hint at the wine's evolution in bottle, adding complexity and sophistication. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: bodegas Valduero  Reserva Premium " 6 Anos"
short_description: Elegant Ribera del Duero Tempranillo with 6 years aging. Ripe dark fruit, cedar, and leather complexity.
description: Bodegas Valduero's Reserva Premium represents the producer's commitment to traditional Ribera del Duero winemaking. This 2016 vintage, aged six years in oak, displays the hallmark character of mature Tempranillo: deep ripe dark fruit on the nose with emerging secondary notes of cedar and vanilla oak. The palate is well-balanced and smooth, with medium tannins that frame red cherry and black plum flavors. Leather and spice notes develop on the finish, reflecting the wine's extended aging and complexity. A refined expression suited to special occasions and food-forward dining.

name: Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite)  Los Vascos Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Prestigious Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—elegant Colchagua expression.
description: Los Vascos Cabernet Sauvignon from Colchagua Valley represents the Domaines Barons de Rothschild commitment to quality winemaking in Chile's premier red wine region. This full-bodied expression showcases dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedary oak and subtle vanilla from careful barrel aging. High, well-integrated tannins provide structure and aging potential, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes add complexity and sophistication. The wine balances New World fruit richness with Old World elegance, making it an excellent choice for serious collectors and food-pairing enthusiasts.

name: Los Vascos Cromas Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Elegant Colchagua expression from Rothschild's portfolio.
description:
Los Vascos Cromas Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon represents a refined expression of Colchagua Valley terroir under the stewardship of Domaines Barons de Rothschild. This full-bodied wine showcases dominant blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit, complemented by pronounced cedar and oak secondary notes that speak to careful barrel aging. Tobacco and leather tertiary characteristics add complexity and age-worthiness.

The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity provide excellent grip and food compatibility. Ideal for cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial red meat dishes, braised preparations, and aged cheeses.

name: San Marzano  Santoro Primitivo Puglia IGP
short_description: Smooth, fruit-driven Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, smoke, and peppery spice. Full-bodied, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description: San Marzano's Santoro Primitivo is a bold, approachable red from Italy's sun-drenched Puglia region. The wine opens with a generous dark-fruit nose—ripe cherry and raspberry—followed by a palate that balances richness with elegant restraint. Smoke and black pepper notes add complexity without overshadowing the fruit; fully rounded tannins deliver a smooth, satisfying finish. This is a wine built for the table: equally at home with pizza and pasta, grilled meats, or casual entertaining. A reliable, well-made expression of Primitivo's warm-climate character.

name: Earthworks  Cabernet Sauvignon (Barossa Valley)
short_description: Full-bodied Barossa Cabernet with blackcurrant intensity, cedar oak, and structured tannins for serious red-meat dining.
description: Earthworks Cabernet Sauvignon from Barossa Valley presents a classic full-bodied expression of Australia's flagship red varietal. Dark cassis and blackcurrant dominate the primary aromatics, supported by structured tannins and medium-high acidity that frame the wine's architecture. Oak aging contributes cedar, vanilla, and subtle smoke notes, while tertiary development reveals tobacco leaf, leather, and earthy minerality. The wine's weight and tannin grip suit grilled steaks, lamb preparations, and slow-braised beef dishes. A food-friendly yet serious red for the collector seeking regional character and cellaring potential.

name: Viu Manent  Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Single-vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from Colchagua Valley with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins.
description: Viu Manent's Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and black cherry fruit, supported by cedar and herbaceous notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannins provide structure without overwhelming the palate, making this a versatile food wine. Cocoa and black pepper spice add complexity, with subtle tobacco undertones suggesting modest aging potential.

name: Viu Manent  Secreto Carmenere
short_description: Elegant Chilean Carmenere with dark fruit, spice, and herbaceous depth—food-friendly and approachable.
description: Viu Manent's Secreto Carmenere represents the signature character of Chile's flagship red varietal. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice. The palate is medium-full and structured, with ripe tannins that frame cocoa and subtle vanilla notes from careful oak aging. Leather and earthy minerality emerge on the finish, adding complexity and food-pairing versatility. This wine bridges approachability with depth—ideal for grilled meats, braised dishes, and mushroom-forward cuisine.

name: Angove  Family Crest Mclaren Vale Shiraz
short_description: Organic McLaren Vale Shiraz with ripe dark berries, spice, and elegant structure—a food-friendly premium expression.
description: Angove Family Crest McLaren Vale Shiraz represents the pinnacle of South Australia's organic winemaking heritage. This medium-full bodied single varietal showcases the coastal Mediterranean terroir of McLaren Vale through a vibrant palate of dark plum, blackberry, and black cherry fruit. Subtle spice and earthy undertones provide complexity, while elegantly structured tannins ensure food-friendly versatility. The wine's organic provenance reflects Angove's pioneering commitment to sustainable viticulture as the country's largest certified organic producer.

name: Luccarelli  Primitivo Puglia IGP
short_description: Smooth, fruit-driven Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, smoke, and pepper—a food-friendly red at an excellent value.
description: Luccarelli's Primitivo from Puglia delivers a bold, fruit-forward profile anchored by ripe dark cherry and raspberry. The palate shows a harmonious blend of dark fruit with supporting notes of smoke and black pepper, underpinned by fully rounded, medium tannins that provide structure without harshness. This is an eminently drinkable wine—smooth, approachable, and rich enough to pair with pizza, pasta, and grilled meats. At 900 THB, it represents strong value for a medium-full-bodied red with genuine complexity and polish.

name: Wolf Blass  Eaglehawk Merlot
short_description: Barossa Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable and food-friendly.
description: Wolf Blass Eaglehawk Merlot from Barossa Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine centred on ripe plum and berry fruit. The palate shows juicy red-fruit character with hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by well-integrated tannins and a touch of vanilla complexity. Soft texture and balanced acidity make this an accessible choice for everyday enjoyment and versatile food pairing.

name: De Bortoli  Sacred Hill Cabernet Merlot
short_description: Approachable Cabernet-Merlot blend from Riverina with ripe dark fruit and soft tannins; food-friendly everyday red.

The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and plum aromatics, supported by subtle oak and vanilla notes from winemaking. Medium-full body and soft tannin structure make it an accessible choice for casual dining and everyday occasions. The blend's natural affinity for grilled meats, braised beef, and tomato-based dishes reflects its food-friendly profile.

name: Jim Barry  The Lodge Hill Shiraz
short_description: Clare Valley Shiraz with ripe dark fruit, peppery spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly Australian classic.

The 2017 vintage delivers ripe blackberry and dark plum on the nose, supported by subtle oak and spice from winemaking. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with emerging leather and earthy notes that suggest complexity to come. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and hearty fare.

name: Howard Park  Flint Rock Shiraz
short_description: Margaret River Syrah with ripe blackberry, pepper spice, and structured tannins. Full-bodied and food-friendly.
description: Howard Park's Flint Rock Shiraz is a full-bodied Margaret River red built on ripe blackberry and dark plum fruit, layered with black pepper spice and subtle cedar notes. Medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential, while secondary smoke and licorice add complexity. A classic Australian Syrah suited to grilled meats, lamb, and hearty braised dishes.

name: Kaiken  Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description: Full-bodied Mendoza Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins—classic Argentine power.
description: Kaiken Estate Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza showcases the region's signature depth and structure. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and black pepper complexity. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide a firm backbone, with tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerging as the wine opens. name: Kaiken  Estate Malbec
short_description: Argentine Malbec with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa; medium-full body and smooth tannins. Elegant and food-friendly.
description:
Kaiken Estate Malbec from Mendoza showcases the signature depth and elegance of Argentina's flagship red varietal. Aromas of blackberry and dark plum dominate the nose, complemented by cocoa, vanilla, and subtle spice from French oak aging. On the palate, medium-full body and well-integrated tannins create a smooth, structured wine with layers of dark fruit and earthy minerality. The finish is clean and persistent, with lingering notes of violet and cocoa.

This wine pairs beautifully with grilled red meats, braised beef dishes, and lamb, as well as aged cheeses and dark chocolate desserts.

name: Kaiken  Terroir Series Malbec/ Bonarda/ Petit Verdot

Aromas of blackberry and plum lead to a medium-full palate with cocoa and violet undertones, supported by well-integrated tannins and subtle earth. Oak aging adds vanilla and spice complexity without overwhelming the fruit. A versatile, approachable red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and casual entertaining.

name: Ca' Momi  Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon

Medium-full body and balanced tannins provide structure without heaviness, making it approachable in youth while retaining aging potential. Vanilla and cedar notes from oak aging add complexity, while hints of cocoa and black pepper round out the palate. name: Merryvale  Napa Valley Merlot
short_description: Napa Valley Merlot with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly structure—a classic expression of California's signature varietal.

The wine opens with dominant ripe black cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle cocoa and oak undertones that reflect careful winemaking. Medium-full body and approachable tannin structure make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or roasted poultry. name: Mollydooker  'Enchanted Path' Shiraz Cabernet - McLaren Vale

name: Wither Hills  Pinot Noir

Medium-full body and refined tannin structure make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or tomato-based pasta. name: De Bortoli  Sacred Hill Shiraz

name: De Bortoli  DB Family Selection Shiraz
short_description: Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with dark fruit, pepper spice, and smoky complexity. Food-friendly everyday red.

name: De Bortoli  Woodfired Shiraz
short_description: Australian Shiraz with dark fruit, smoke, and spice—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and BBQ.

name: Jacob's Creek  Double Barrel Shiraz (750 ml)
short_description: Full-bodied Barossa Shiraz with dark fruit, oak, and peppery spice—a versatile red for fine dining and hearty fare.
description: Jacob's Creek Double Barrel Shiraz from Barossa Valley delivers a full-bodied expression of Australia's signature varietal. Dark fruit—blackberry and plum—dominates the palate, supported by vanilla and cocoa from oak maturation. Black pepper and spice notes add complexity and structure, while subtle smoke rounds the finish. This wine balances approachability with depth, making it equally at home at the dinner table or paired with grilled meats and slow-cooked dishes.

name: St. Hugo  Coonawarra Cabernet Sauvignon (750 ml)
short_description: Structured Coonawarra Cabernet with blackcurrant intensity, mint freshness, and elegant cedar complexity.
description: St. Hugo's Coonawarra Cabernet Sauvignon is a full-bodied expression of Australia's premier red wine region. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by distinctive mint and eucalyptus notes characteristic of Coonawarra terroir. Tertiary leather and slate notes emerge with bottle age, creating a wine built for cellaring and pairing with substantial dishes.

name: Campo Viejo  Tempranillo (750 ml)
short_description: Bold Rioja Tempranillo with dark plum, cherry, and spice—a food-friendly classic with excellent value.
description: Campo Viejo's Tempranillo is a quintessential Rioja expression: dry, bold, and structured with medium-full body and medium-high acidity. name: Campo Viejo  Reserva (750 ml)

The wine displays a deep ruby color and opens with aromas of dark plum and red cherry, complemented by leather and dried herb notes. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced acidity frame flavors of ripe stone fruit, vanilla oak, and subtle tobacco, with a long, structured finish. This is a classically styled Reserva built for food pairing and cellar aging.

name: Brancott Estate  Pinot Noir (750 ml)
short_description: Marlborough Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh summer character—versatile and food-friendly.
description: Brancott Estate's Marlborough Pinot Noir delivers the region's signature elegance: bright red fruit and raspberry on the nose, with a fresh, silky palate that avoids heaviness. Medium-high acidity and restrained tannin structure make this wine approachable young, yet layered enough for thoughtful pairing. Subtle earthy notes add complexity without dominating. A versatile choice for warm-weather entertaining and lighter protein dishes.

name: Bodegas Volver  Volver Single Vineyard
short_description: Single-vineyard Tempranillo from 1957-planted vines at Finca Los Juncares, 700 m, unfiltered after 14–15 months in French oak barriques.
description:
When Rafael Cañizares founded Bodegas Volver in 2004, the explicit ambition was to prove that DO La Mancha — Spain's bulk-wine engine — could produce single-vineyard Tempranillo as site-specific as anything from Ribera del Duero. The proof is Finca Los Juncares.

This 29-hectare plot in Cuenca province sits at 700 m elevation on poor sandy soils over clay subsoil with limestone patches, dry-farmed and organically managed with a punishing 242 mm average annual rainfall. The altitude delivers the diurnal swings — up to 18–20°C between day and night — that preserve acidity in fruit ripened under intense continental heat. Vines planted in 1957 push roots deep into the subsoil for water, yielding small, concentrated berries.

In the cellar, hand-harvested grapes are sorted on a selection table before primary fermentation in open-top stainless steel tanks at ~28°C with indigenous yeast. Malolactic fermentation is completed in barrel, followed by 14–15 months in new and second-fill French oak barriques (225 L). The wine is bottled unfiltered at approximately 15% ABV.

What sets this apart: it is one of the only unfiltered, single-vineyard Tempranillos from La Mancha aged with in-barrel malolactic — a Bordeaux-influenced technique almost unheard of in this DO. The current vintage shows the hallmark of slow ripening under altitude-moderated heat: dense fruit concentration alongside structural acidity that extends the drinking window well beyond what La Mancha's reputation would suggest.

name: Bodegas Protos  Roble Ribera del Duero D.O.
short_description: Smooth, fruit-forward Tempranillo from Ribiera del Duero with ripe dark berries, balanced tannins, and approachable oak complexity.
description: Bodegas Protos Roble represents the classic expression of Ribiera del Duero Tempranillo—a wine built on ripe dark-fruit intensity and structural balance. The palate shows prominent blackcurrant and black cherry, supported by medium tannins that remain polished rather than aggressive. Oak aging contributes subtle vanilla and spice notes without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. The wine's well-balanced profile and smooth finish make it an ideal introduction to the region's signature style, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or casual dining.

name: Bodegas Protos  Crianza Ribera del Duero D.O.
short_description: Smooth, fruit-forward Tempranillo from Ribera del Duero with ripe dark berries, oak complexity, and elegant balance.
description:
Bodegas Protos Crianza represents the classic expression of Ribera del Duero's Tempranillo tradition. This medium-full-bodied red displays dominant ripe blackcurrant and black cherry on the nose, with supporting oak and vanilla from careful aging. The palate is well-balanced and approachable, with smooth tannins and a marked finish that lingers pleasantly. Subtle spice and earth notes emerge on the close, adding complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character.

A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and roasted poultry, or enjoyed on its own as an elegant aperitif.

name: Henschke  Henry's Seven, Barossa Valley
short_description: Barossa GSM-led blend from the sixth-generation Henschke family; open-top fermented, screwcap-sealed, built for immediate pleasure without sacrificing site character.
description: Henry's Seven takes its name from the seven Barossa Valley growers whose parcels Stephen Henschke has sourced and blended across successive vintages — a deliberate counterpoint to the family's obsessive single-vineyard philosophy, designed to capture the breadth of the valley floor rather than the depth of one site. The Barossa Valley floor delivers warm days and cool nights moderated by the Southern Ocean, with alluvial red-brown earths and sandy loam over clay that concentrate dark fruit while retaining enough natural acidity to keep the blend from heaviness. Fermentation takes place in open-top vessels, consistent with the Henschke house method, with full malolactic fermentation completed before the wine moves to seasoned oak — no new oak is used at this tier, preserving the fruit-forward character the blend demands. No tannin additions are made, a non-negotiable house rule across all Henschke labels regardless of price point. What sets Henry's Seven apart within the Barossa GSM category is its provenance: it is made under the same winemaking hand and the same no-additions discipline as Hill of Grace, simply drawing on multi-grower Barossa fruit rather than a single pre-phylloxera block. The 2023 vintage benefits from Barossa's characteristically generous growing season, delivering plush dark-berry concentration with the structural restraint that Henschke's biodynamic farming philosophy consistently achieves across its portfolio.

name: Yalumba  Distinguished Sites Paradox Shiraz
short_description: Barossa floor Shiraz from Yalumba's Distinguished Sites tier — wild ferment, in-house cooperage oak, vegan since 2012.
description: Founded in 1849 by Samuel Smith and still in the hands of the sixth-generation Hill-Smith family, Yalumba operates the only winery-owned cooperage in the Southern Hemisphere — a fact that gives every barrel in this cellar a provenance no peer in Australia can replicate.
