short_description: Elegant Piedmont sparkling with Pinot Blanc and Chardonnay; crisp apple, citrus, and brioche complexity.
description: Batasiolo's Pinot Chardonnay Spumante is a refined Italian sparkling wine from Piedmont, blending 60% Pinot Blanc with 40% Chardonnay. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest aromatics, followed by delicate brioche and stone fruit notes on the palate. Fine bubbles and vibrant acidity create a refreshing, elegant mouthfeel with subtle minerality and floral undertones. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, oysters, and fresh cheeses.

name: Jansz  Tasmania Premium Cuvée
short_description: Tasmanian sparkling Pinot Noir with vibrant red fruit, crisp acidity, and mineral freshness—ideal for celebrations and seafood.
description:
Jansz Tasmania Premium Cuvée is a non-vintage sparkling wine crafted from Pinot Noir, capturing the cool-climate character of Tasmania's premium wine region. The wine displays a light, elegant body with pronounced high acidity and a silky texture that defines modern Australian sparkling production.

On the nose and palate, red fruit—particularly raspberry—dominates the primary impression, complemented by fresh citrus and stone fruit notes. A subtle mineral undertone adds complexity and food-friendliness. The wine's crisp, refreshing profile makes it an excellent aperitif and a versatile partner to seafood, oysters, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Montelvini Prosecco Treviso Doc Brut (Serenitatis Collection)
Domaine Coustarret Elevation Pet Nat
short_description: Gros Manseng pet-nat with vibrant citrus, brioche, and minerality—a lively, food-friendly sparkling wine.
description: Domaine Coustarret's Elevation Pet Nat showcases the distinctive character of Gros Manseng in a naturally carbonated format. The wine displays a pale golden hue with fine, persistent bubbles characteristic of the pet-nat method. On the nose, green apple and citrus zest lead, followed by delicate honeysuckle and brioche notes. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with stone fruit undertones and a refreshing finish that invites another sip. This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine suited to casual entertaining and seafood-forward occasions.

name: Oddbird Blanc de Blancs Sparkling France (Non-Alcoholic)
Craggy Range  Te Muna Sauvignon Blanc Martinborough
short_description: Vibrant Sauvignon Blanc from Martinborough with citrus zest, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description: Craggy Range's Te Muna Sauvignon Blanc exemplifies the Martinborough terroir through a refreshing white wine marked by bright citrus zest, herbaceous character, and mineral-driven complexity. The wine balances green apple and lime notes with subtle tropical fruit undertones, delivering medium body and crisp acidity that invites food pairing across seafood, salads, and Asian cuisine.

name: Michel Lynch  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Bordeaux Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness—ideal for seafood and Asian cuisine.
description: Michel Lynch Sauvignon Blanc is a classic expression of the Bordeaux white tradition, delivering bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose with herbaceous undertones. The palate reveals tropical fruit character balanced by crisp acidity and subtle minerality, creating a refreshing, food-friendly profile. Medium body and clean finish make this wine versatile for casual dining and entertaining.

name: Catena Zapata  Alamos Chardonnay
short_description: Mendoza Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus brightness, and subtle oak complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Catena Zapata's Alamos Chardonnay from Mendoza showcases the classic profile of Argentina's premier white wine region. Ripe stone fruit and citrus dominate the nose, with green apple freshness on entry. Oak aging contributes subtle butter and vanilla notes, while a mineral backbone and hazelnut undertone add complexity and structure. Medium body and crisp acidity make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with seafood, poultry, or creamy pasta dishes.

name: Fantinel  Sant' Helena Pinot Grigio
short_description: Crisp Friuli Pinot Grigio with citrus zest, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description: Fantinel's Sant' Helena Pinot Grigio represents the classic expression of this varietal from Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Italy's premier white wine region. The wine displays a pale straw colour with green reflections, offering a fresh and inviting aromatic profile dominated by citrus zest, green apple, and subtle stone fruit notes. On the palate, medium body and brisk acidity create a clean, refreshing mouthfeel with mineral undertones and a crisp, lingering finish. This is an ideal aperitif wine and pairs beautifully with grilled fish, shellfish, fresh salads, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Paul Jaboulet  Parallele 45 White
short_description: Elegant Rhône white blend of Grenache Blanc, Marsanne, Viognier & Bourboulenc—stone fruit, citrus, and floral minerality.
description:
Paul Jaboulet's Parallele 45 is a refined white blend from the southern Rhône Valley, combining four complementary varietals in a harmonious composition. The blend of 50% Grenache Blanc, 20% Marsanne, 20% Viognier, and 10% Bourboulenc creates a wine of medium body with bright, medium-high acidity that refreshes the palate.

On the nose and palate, expect stone fruit and citrus zest as the dominant impression, supported by delicate floral and herbaceous notes with a mineral backbone. The wine's structure and freshness make it an ideal aperitif or food companion for lighter dishes, particularly those with Asian spice, grilled preparations, or seafood.

name: Craggy Range  Chardonnay Kidnappers Vineyard
short_description: Premium New Zealand Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak complexity from Hawke's Bay's Kidnappers Vineyard.
description: Craggy Range's Chardonnay from the Kidnappers Vineyard in Hawke's Bay represents the house style of refined, food-friendly white wine from New Zealand's premier cool-climate region. The wine displays a harmonious balance of ripe stone fruit—peach and apricot—with bright citrus zest and a mineral backbone. Oak aging contributes subtle butter and hazelnut notes without overwhelming the fruit, while a touch of brioche complexity suggests careful winemaking. Medium-full body and medium-high acidity make this an elegant aperitif or versatile table wine suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Errazuriz  Estate Reserva Chardonnay
short_description: Aconcagua Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and creamy oak—elegant Chilean expression.
description: Errazuriz Estate Reserva Chardonnay from Chile's Aconcagua Valley delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and restrained acidity. The primary character centers on green apple and bright citrus zest, supported by ripe stone fruit notes that give the wine approachability and depth. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and subtle toast, creating a creamy mid-palate without overwhelming the fruit. A mineral backbone emerges on the finish, suggesting terroir influence and aging potential. This is a food-friendly Chardonnay suited to rich preparations and seafood alike.

name: Sileni Estates  Cellar Selection Sauvignon Blanc Marlborough
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with tropical fruit, citrus zest, and herbaceous minerality.
description: A classic expression of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc, this wine showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Grapefruit and tropical fruit dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones that speak to the cool-climate terroir. On the palate, crisp citrus zest and green apple provide structure and freshness, with a mineral backbone that lingers through the finish. The light body and high acidity make this an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Two Oceans  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp South African Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description: Two Oceans Sauvignon Blanc from Western Cape delivers a classic expression of the varietal with bright citrus zest and herbaceous character. The wine's high acidity and light body make it an ideal aperitif and food companion, particularly suited to seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine. Minerality and green apple notes provide complexity on the palate, while a clean, refreshing finish invites another sip.

name: Tyrrell's  Chardonnay "Old Winery"
short_description: Crisp Hunter Valley white blend of Semillon and Sauvignon Blanc with citrus, stone fruit, and herbaceous character.
description: Tyrrell's Old Winery Chardonnay is a medium-bodied white wine from Australia's Hunter Valley, crafted from a blend of Semillon and Sauvignon Blanc. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and stone-fruit aromatics, supported by herbaceous notes and subtle minerality. A crisp, refreshing palate with green-apple acidity makes this wine an ideal aperitif or food companion for seafood, light pasta, and Asian cuisine.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Eden Valley Sauvignon Blanc from Australia's oldest family winery, cool-fermented for aromatic precision at 400–550 m elevation.
description: Founded in 1849 by Samuel Smith and still held by the sixth-generation Hill-Smith family, Yalumba built its reputation on terroir transparency at scale — a philosophy that extends even to its entry-tier Y Series. Eden Valley fruit, sourced from rocky, shallow, well-drained soils at 400–550 m elevation, delivers the natural acidity and taut fruit definition that warmer Barossa floor sites cannot replicate. The altitude compresses the diurnal range, slowing ripening and locking in primary aromatic compounds. Fermentation is conducted in stainless steel under cool, controlled temperatures, preserving volatile thiols and green-herb aromatics that are the varietal signature. The house's vegan certification (no animal-derived fining agents since the 2012 vintage) applies across all tiers, including this one. What distinguishes the Y Series within its price bracket is the institutional knowledge behind it: the same Yalumba Nursery that imported and trialled Viognier clones in 1980 — decades before any Australian peer — informs the clonal selection and site matching that underpins even this commercial expression. Drink young, within 1–2 years of vintage, to capture the elevation-driven freshness at its most vivid.

name: Wither Hills  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness.
description: Wither Hills Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough, New Zealand's premier white wine region, delivers a refreshing expression of the varietal. The wine showcases bright citrus zest and herbaceous character on the nose, with green apple and tropical fruit notes on the palate. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or food companion, particularly with seafood, fresh salads, and Asian cuisine.

name: Tahuna  Pinot Gris Hawke's Bay
short_description: Crisp Pinot Grigio from Hawke's Bay with vibrant citrus and green apple; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description: Tahuna's Pinot Grigio from Hawke's Bay delivers the varietal's signature brightness and mineral precision. Dominant citrus zest and green apple notes define the primary character, supported by subtle stone fruit undertones that add depth without heaviness. The medium body and lively acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for delicate seafood, fresh oysters, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Tahuna  Chardonnay Hawke's Bay
short_description: Hawke's Bay Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and elegant oak—versatile with seafood and cream sauces.
description: Tahuna's Hawke's Bay Chardonnay showcases the region's signature balance of fruit ripeness and mineral precision. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit and subtle floral notes. On the palate, vanilla oak and butter add richness without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity, while toasted hazelnut and minerality provide complexity. Medium-Full body and restrained tannin make this wine approachable yet structured—ideal for seafood, roasted poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Tahuna  Sauvignon Blanc Marlborough
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with grapefruit, tropical notes, and herbaceous snap—crisp aperitif or seafood companion.
description: Tahuna Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough delivers the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Grapefruit and tropical fruit dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous minerality and citrus zest on the palate. The wine's lean structure and clean finish make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, grilled fish, and spicy Asian cuisine.

name: Langmeil  Spring Fever Chardonnay
short_description: Barossa Valley Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and creamy oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Langmeil's Spring Fever Chardonnay is a medium-bodied white from Australia's renowned Barossa Valley. The wine opens with bright stone fruit and citrus aromatics, supported by creamy oak and subtle vanilla notes that reflect careful winemaking. Medium-high acidity provides freshness and structure, making this a versatile companion to seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes. The minerality adds complexity and length on the finish.

name: Rocca Di Frassinello  Vermentino IGT
short_description: Crisp Tuscan Vermentino with citrus zest, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—refreshing coastal white.
description: Rocca Di Frassinello's Vermentino IGT is a single-varietal expression from Tuscany showcasing the grape's signature coastal character. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous notes. A pronounced minerality and sea salt finish provide structure and food-friendly acidity, making this a versatile aperitif and seafood companion. Medium body and crisp acidity define a refreshing, approachable profile suited to warm-weather dining.

name: Weingut Robert Weil  Riesling Kiedricher Trocken
short_description: Rheingau Riesling Trocken: crisp citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision. Dry, refreshing, food-friendly.
description: Weingut Robert Weil's Kiedricher Trocken is a dry Riesling from the prestigious Rheingau region, showcasing the varietal's classic balance of fruit and acidity. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and a distinctive mineral backbone characteristic of the region's slate-rich terroir. The herbaceous undertones and lime notes add complexity and freshness, making this a versatile table wine suited to a wide range of cuisines, particularly those with acidity or spice.

name: Cloudy Bay  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus drive, herbaceous snap, and mineral precision—a classic expression of New Zealand terroir.
description: Cloudy Bay's 2024 Sauvignon Blanc exemplifies the signature profile of Marlborough's cool-climate whites: a light-bodied wine with piercing acidity and a crystalline mineral backbone. The primary aromatics deliver grapefruit, lime, and herbaceous green notes, with supporting tropical and green apple undertones that add complexity without weight. The palate is clean and focused, with a saline minerality that lingers through the finish, making this wine an ideal aperitif or food companion for seafood, fresh cheeses, and bright vegetable-forward dishes.

name: Cloudy Bay  Chardonnay
short_description: Marlborough Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Cloudy Bay's 2022 Chardonnay from Marlborough showcases the region's signature brightness and mineral precision. Stone fruit and citrus dominate the nose, with green apple and peach on the palate, complemented by restrained oak-derived butter and vanilla. Medium body and crisp acidity make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with seafood, poultry, or creamy pasta dishes.

name: Delheim  Chenin Blanc
short_description: Crisp Stellenbosch Chenin Blanc with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—versatile and food-friendly.
description:
Delheim's Chenin Blanc from Stellenbosch showcases the varietal's signature balance of freshness and complexity. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, followed by stone fruit and subtle honey notes that add depth without heaviness. A mineral backbone and floral undertones provide sophistication, while the medium body and brisk acidity make it refreshing on the palate and remarkably versatile at the table.

This is a wine that performs equally well as an aperitif, alongside seafood, or paired with Asian cuisine where its acidity cuts through spice and richness.

name: Martinborough  Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc Te Tera
short_description: Vibrant Sauvignon Blanc from Martinborough with citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness.
description: A classic expression of New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc from the Martinborough region, this wine showcases the varietal's signature brightness and aromatic intensity. Expect a refreshing profile dominated by citrus zest, green apple, and herbaceous notes, with supporting tropical fruit and minerality that reflect the region's cool-climate terroir. The medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif and food companion for seafood, fresh vegetables, and Asian cuisine.

name: Fromm Winery  Chardonnay La strada
short_description: Marlborough Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Fromm Winery's Chardonnay La Strada is a refined expression of New Zealand's Marlborough region, showcasing the varietal's classic balance of ripe stone fruit and bright citrus. The palate reveals layers of green apple and minerality, underpinned by restrained oak that contributes butter and vanilla notes without overwhelming the fruit. A whisper of hazelnut and brioche on the finish adds complexity and elegance, making this wine a versatile companion to seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Lion's Cape  Coastal White Chardonnay (Fruit wine blend)
description: Lion's Cape  Coastal White Chardonnay (Fruit wine blend)

name: Mud House  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, tropical notes, and herbaceous minerality. Crisp, food-friendly, and refreshing.
description: Mudhouse Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough, New Zealand's premier white wine region, delivers a classic expression of the varietal. Bright citrus zest and tropical fruit dominate the nose, with supporting herbaceous and mineral notes that define the region's signature style. On the palate, medium body and brisk acidity create a refreshing, clean finish ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Los Vascos Chardonnay
short_description: Elegant Chilean Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes.
description: Los Vascos Chardonnay from Colchagua Valley showcases the signature balance of Chile's premier white wine terroir. Medium-full bodied with restrained acidity, this wine opens with green apple and citrus zest, evolving to ripe stone fruit on the mid-palate. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and toasted hazelnut notes without overwhelming the fruit. A mineral backbone and subtle complexity make it an excellent pairing for shellfish, roasted chicken, cream-based risotto, and aged hard cheeses. Domaines Barons de Rothschild's stewardship ensures consistent quality and food-friendly structure.

name: Viu Manent  Secreto Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Casablanca Sauvignon Blanc with zesty citrus, herbaceous snap, and mineral precision—a crisp aperitif and seafood companion.
description: Viu Manent's Secreto Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Casablanca Valley delivers the region's signature brightness and focus. Light-bodied with piercing acidity, this wine showcases vivid citrus zest, herbaceous character, and green apple on the nose and palate. Mineral undertones and subtle tropical notes add complexity without weight, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for raw seafood, grilled fish, and fresh cheeses.

name: Trimbach  Pinot Blanc
short_description: Crisp Alsatian Pinot Blanc with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral elegance—refreshing and food-friendly.
description: Trimbach's Pinot Blanc exemplifies the refined, mineral-driven style of Alsatian white wine. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by delicate stone fruit and subtle floral notes. A clean, refreshing palate with medium body and vibrant acidity makes this an ideal aperitif or versatile food companion. The mineral backbone and almond undertones add complexity without heaviness, characteristic of quality Alsatian Pinot Blanc.

name: Trimbach  Riesling
short_description: Crisp Alsatian Riesling with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision. High acidity and elegant balance.
description:
Trimbach Riesling represents the classic dry expression of Alsace's signature varietal. This wine displays a light, crystalline body with pronounced acidity that defines its structure and food-friendliness. Stone fruit and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by subtle floral and mineral notes that speak to the terroir of the region.


The palate is clean and precise, with green apple and lime acidity driving the finish. Subtle petrol and wet-stone undertones emerge with aeration, adding complexity and aging potential. An exemplary aperitif wine and versatile food companion.

name: Trimbach  Gewurztraminer
short_description: Aromatic Alsatian Gewurztraminer with lychee, rose, and spice—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Trimbach's Gewurztraminer exemplifies the classic Alsatian expression of this aromatic white varietal. The wine opens with pronounced floral and lychee aromatics, layered with white pepper spice and hints of honey. Medium body and brisk acidity deliver freshness and balance, making it equally at home as an apéritif or paired with aromatic Asian cuisine. Stone fruit undertones and subtle spice complexity reward contemplative sipping.

name: De Bortoli  Sacred Hill Semillon Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Australian Semillon-Sauvignon Blanc blend with citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description: De Bortoli's Sacred Hill Semillon Sauvignon Blanc is a vibrant white blend from Australia's Riverina region, crafted to deliver immediate refreshment and versatility at the table. The marriage of Semillon's stone-fruit character and Sauvignon Blanc's herbaceous, citrus-driven profile creates a wine with bright acidity and mineral undertones. On the nose, expect green apple, citrus zest, and subtle tropical hints; the palate is clean and crisp, with a lingering finish that invites another sip. This is an approachable, food-friendly white suited to warm-weather entertaining and casual dining.

name: Domaine Albert Mann  Pinot Gris
short_description: Elegant Alsatian Pinot Gris with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision. Refreshing and versatile.
description: Domaine Albert Mann's 2022 Pinot Gris exemplifies the refined character of Alsatian white wine. This single-varietal expression balances ripe stone fruit with crisp citrus and a subtle mineral backbone, delivering a medium-bodied wine with vibrant acidity. The aromatic profile suggests pear and green apple on the nose, with white pepper spice and delicate floral notes emerging on the palate. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, light pasta, and Asian cuisine.

name: Domaine Ostertag  Clos Mathis - Riesling
short_description: Elegant Alsatian Riesling with vibrant stone fruit, citrus minerality, and crisp acidity—a versatile food wine.
description: Domaine Ostertag's Clos Mathis Riesling exemplifies the precision and mineral-driven character of Alsatian white winemaking. The 2021 vintage delivers a light-bodied expression with pronounced stone fruit aromatics, citrus zest, and a distinctive mineral backbone that speaks to the terroir. High acidity provides freshness and food-friendliness, while subtle floral and petrol notes add complexity. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with raw oysters, grilled white fish, or spiced Asian cuisine.

name: Blue Nun  Riesling
short_description: Crisp German Riesling with citrus, stone fruit, and floral notes—refreshing and food-friendly.
description: Blue Nun Riesling from Rheinhessen delivers a classic expression of Germany's most celebrated white varietal. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, followed by stone fruit and delicate floral aromatics. On the palate, medium body and vibrant acidity create a refreshing, balanced profile with subtle honey and minerality on the finish. A versatile, approachable wine suited to casual dining and aperitif service.

name: Villa Maria  Private Bin Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, tropical notes, and crisp minerality. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description: Villa Maria's Private Bin Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Expect dominant citrus zest and tropical fruit on the nose, with herbaceous undertones and green apple on the palate. The wine's medium body and mineral finish make it an ideal aperitif and versatile food companion, particularly with seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Villa Maria  Private Bin Chardonnay
short_description: Marlborough Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Villa Maria's Private Bin Chardonnay from Marlborough showcases the region's signature stone fruit and citrus character. Medium-bodied with bright acidity, this wine balances primary notes of peach and green apple with secondary oak-derived butter and vanilla complexity. A versatile expression suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: De Bortoli  Sacred Hill Chardonnay
short_description: Riverina Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description: De Bortoli's Sacred Hill Chardonnay from Riverina showcases the region's warm-climate fruit character. Green apple and citrus zest lead the nose, followed by ripe peach and stone fruit on the palate. Winemaking influence appears as vanilla, butter, and toasted oak—hallmarks of barrel aging that add texture and complexity. A whisper of minerality and hazelnut in the finish suggests subtle tertiary development. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with creamy pasta, roasted chicken, or grilled seafood.

name: De Bortoli  DB Family Selection Chardonnay
short_description: Riverina Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile, food-friendly, and refreshingly crisp.
description: De Bortoli's DB Family Selection Chardonnay from Riverina showcases the region's ability to produce balanced, approachable white wines. A blend of 90% Chardonnay and 10% Colombard, this wine delivers ripe stone fruit and citrus on the nose, with a medium body and bright acidity that keeps it lively on the palate. Subtle oak and butter notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit, while a clean, mineral finish invites another sip. Ideal for casual dining, seafood, and creamy pasta dishes.

name: Brancott Estate  Sauvignon Blanc (750 ml)
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, herbaceous snap, and tropical undertones.
description: A classic expression of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc, this wine delivers bright grapefruit and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous minerality and tropical fruit notes on the palate. High acidity and a light, crisp body make it an ideal aperitif and a versatile food companion for seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Brancott Estate  Letter Series "B" Sauvignon Blanc (750 ml)
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description:
Brancott Estate's Letter Series "B" Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. The wine opens with grapefruit and green apple on the nose, followed by herbaceous and grassy undertones that reflect the cool-climate terroir. On the palate, tropical and citrus-zest notes emerge, balanced by crisp minerality and a clean, refreshing finish.

This light-bodied white is an excellent aperitif and pairs exceptionally well with oysters, grilled fish, fresh salads, and goat cheese. Its high acidity also makes it a natural companion to spicy Asian cuisine, particularly Thai dishes.

name: Apopo  Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness—a crisp, food-friendly white.
description: Apopo's Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc exemplifies the region's signature style: a medium-bodied white with pronounced medium-high acidity and a clean, refreshing profile. The wine opens with herbaceous and citrus-zest aromatics, followed by green apple and tropical fruit on the palate, underpinned by subtle minerality. A versatile table wine suited to seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Greywacke  Riesling
short_description: Marlborough Riesling with vibrant citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—refreshing and food-friendly.
description: Greywacke's Riesling from Marlborough showcases the region's signature brightness and mineral character. Dominant citrus zest and stone fruit aromas lead to a palate of green apple and lime, underpinned by subtle floral and herbaceous notes. The wine's medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif and versatile food companion, particularly with Asian cuisine, seafood, and fresh preparations.

name: Greywacke  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with zesty citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description: Greywacke's Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature intensity and clarity. Bright citrus zest and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by green apple and tropical fruit notes that evolve with air. A crisp, mineral backbone and refreshing acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or food companion, particularly with seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Bodegas Protos   Verdejo Rueda D.O.
short_description: Crisp Verdejo from Rueda with citrus, green apple, and mineral complexity—a refreshing Spanish white for seafood and aperitifs.
description: Bodegas Protos' Verdejo from the Rueda D.O. is a classic expression of Spain's signature white varietal. This single-varietal wine showcases the characteristic brightness and mineral-driven profile that defines quality Verdejo production. The palate opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous notes that speak to the region's terroir. Medium body and crisp acidity make this wine exceptionally food-friendly and refreshing as an aperitif.

name: Rockford  Local Growers Semillon
short_description: Crisp Barossa Valley Sémillon with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Rockford's 2021 Local Growers Sémillon represents the classic Australian expression of this noble white varietal. Sourced from Barossa Valley vineyards, this wine delivers a refreshing profile anchored by bright citrus zest and stone-fruit notes, complemented by subtle herbaceous and mineral undertones. The medium body and vibrant acidity create a wine of elegance and versatility, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood and light cuisine. A benchmark example of contemporary Barossa white winemaking.

name: Penfolds  Bin 51 Eden Valley Riesling
short_description: Eden Valley Riesling from Australia's coolest high-altitude Riesling corridor — bone-dry, naturally high-acid, built for a decade of cellaring.
description: Penfolds, a house defined by its blending philosophy and red-wine dominance since 1844, makes a deliberate pivot with Bin 51: a single-appellation Eden Valley Riesling that breaks entirely from the multi-regional, oak-driven house template. Eden Valley sits at elevations above 400 metres in the Mount Lofty Ranges, where ancient granite and quartz-rich soils over clay subsoil force vines to dig deep, producing naturally low yields and intensely concentrated fruit with piercing natural acidity. The cool continental nights preserve aromatic tension that the valley floor Barossa, just kilometres below, cannot replicate. Penfolds vinifies Bin 51 in the reductive style characteristic of Australian Riesling: cool-fermented in stainless steel, no oak contact, no malolactic fermentation, bottled early to lock in primary citrus and floral aromatics and the structural acidity that drives long-term development. The result is a wine sealed to evolve — Eden Valley Riesling at this elevation is one of the few Australian whites with a credible 10–15 year cellaring arc, developing classic petrol and lanolin complexity with age. Within the Penfolds portfolio, Bin 51 is the sole expression that foregrounds a single cool-climate appellation over the blended, oak-shaped house style — a structural outlier in every sense. The 2023 vintage in Eden Valley delivered a cool, even growing season with excellent natural acidity retention; drink 2025–2033+.

name: Mount Riley  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus, tropical fruit, and herbaceous minerality—crisp and food-friendly.
description: Mount Riley's Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature aromatic intensity and bright acidity. The wine opens with zesty citrus zest and tropical fruit notes, underpinned by herbaceous minerality and green apple. A refreshing, medium-bodied white with excellent food versatility, it delivers the classic New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc profile: vibrant, clean, and expressive.

name: Mount Riley  17 Valley Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision—crisp and food-friendly.
description: Mount Riley's Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature brightness and aromatic intensity. Expect a medium-bodied white with pronounced citrus zest, herbaceous undertones, and a mineral spine that defines New Zealand's cool-climate expression. Green apple and tropical fruit notes provide complexity, while the medium-high acidity delivers a refreshing, clean finish ideal for seafood, fresh cheese, and vibrant Asian cuisine.

name: Bodegas Garzon  Reserva Albarino
short_description: Crisp Uruguayan Albariño with citrus zest, stone fruit, and saline minerality—a refreshing white for seafood.
description: Bodegas Garzon's Reserva Albariño showcases the versatility of this Spanish-origin varietal in Uruguay's Atlantic-influenced Maldonado region. The wine displays a bright, medium-bodied profile with pronounced medium-high acidity and a clean, mineral-driven palate. Primary notes of citrus zest and green apple lead to stone fruit and peach, underpinned by subtle herbaceous and saline characteristics that reflect the coastal terroir. The finish is crisp and refreshing, making this an ideal aperitif or seafood companion.

name: Wolf Blass  Bilyara Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Barossa Valley Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus and herbaceous notes; refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description: Wolf Blass Bilyara Sauvignon Blanc is a vibrant white wine from Australia's Barossa Valley, crafted from 100% Sauvignon Blanc. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple aromatics, followed by herbaceous and tropical fruit notes that define its primary character. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a crisp, refreshing palate structure ideal for warm-weather enjoyment and food pairing versatility. Subtle minerality and stone fruit undertones add complexity without overwhelming the wine's clean, fruit-forward profile. This expression exemplifies the modern Australian approach to Sauvignon Blanc—approachable, well-balanced, and food-friendly.

name: Wolf Blass  Yellow Label Moscato
short_description: Aromatic Moscato from Barossa Valley with floral, honey, and stone-fruit character. Refreshing, off-dry, and food-friendly.
description: Wolf Blass Yellow Label Moscato is a lightly-bodied, aromatic white wine from Australia's Barossa Valley. The wine showcases the classic Moscato profile: delicate floral notes, honeyed sweetness, and stone-fruit aromatics with hints of lychee and tropical fruit. Medium-high acidity provides freshness and balance, making it approachable and versatile at table. Ideal as an apéritif, with light Asian cuisine, or paired with fruit-based desserts.

name: Wolf Blass  Gold Label Adelaide Hills Chardonnay
short_description: Adelaide Hills Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Wolf Blass Gold Label Adelaide Hills Chardonnay is a classically styled white wine showcasing the cool-climate character of South Australia's Adelaide Hills region. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by ripe stone fruit and a whisper of vanilla oak from careful barrel aging. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced acidity deliver a creamy mouthfeel with toasted hazelnut and subtle spice notes, finishing with mineral precision and lingering citrus. This is a versatile food wine suited to rich seafood, roasted poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Yalumba  Samuel's Collection Eden Valley Chardonnay
short_description: High-altitude Eden Valley Chardonnay from Yalumba's rocky 400–550 m sites — cool-climate precision from Australia's oldest family-owned winery.
description: Founded in 1849 by Samuel Smith, Yalumba has spent six generations learning that Eden Valley's elevation — 400–550 metres above sea level — is where South Australia's white wines find their sharpest definition. The Samuel's Collection Chardonnay draws from these same shallow, rocky, well-drained soils that the house relies on for its benchmark Virgilius Viognier, where diurnal temperature swings compress flavour while preserving natural acidity. The cool-climate growing conditions slow ripening considerably relative to the Barossa floor, producing fruit with tighter structure and more restrained sugar accumulation. Yalumba's winemaking philosophy across all tiers favours minimal intervention, and the house's vegan certification — in place since the 2012 vintage, with no animal-derived fining agents — applies here. The Samuel's Collection tier sits as the entry point into Yalumba's estate Eden Valley range, making high-altitude fruit accessible without the extended oak regimes reserved for the flagship expressions. What distinguishes this expression within its category is the combination of genuine high-elevation Eden Valley provenance at an accessible price point, backed by a producer whose on-site cooperage and viticultural research infrastructure is unmatched in the Southern Hemisphere. Drink now through the near term for freshness, or hold briefly for further textural integration.

name: Vasse Felix  Icon Range Heytesbury Chardonnay
short_description: Margaret River Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and toasted oak complexity.
description: Vasse Felix's Icon Range Heytesbury Chardonnay represents the Margaret River region's signature white wine expression. The 2023 vintage displays a harmonious balance of primary stone fruit and citrus character, complemented by subtle oak influence and minerality. Medium-Full body and restrained acidity create a wine of elegance and food-friendly versatility, suitable for both aperitif enjoyment and pairing with refined cuisine.

name: Vasse Felix  Premier Range Chardonnay
short_description: Margaret River Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and elegant oak—a versatile premium white.
description: Vasse Felix Premier Range Chardonnay is a classically styled white wine from Margaret River, Australia's premier cool-climate region. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by ripe stone fruit and subtle floral notes. On the palate, vanilla oak and butter provide richness and texture, balanced by medium acidity and a mineral backbone. The finish reveals hints of toasted hazelnut and minerality, adding complexity and length. This is a food-friendly Chardonnay suited to fine dining and special occasions.

name: Vasse Felix  Filius Range Chardonnay
short_description: Margaret River Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and toasted oak complexity.
description: Vasse Felix Filius Range Chardonnay is a medium-full bodied white wine from Margaret River, Australia's premier cool-climate region. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by ripe stone fruit character. On the palate, toasted oak and butter notes provide structure and richness, balanced by crisp acidity and subtle minerality. The finish is clean and persistent, with hints of hazelnut and vanilla oak influence.

name: Wolf Blass  Red Label Chardonnay
short_description: Barossa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, buttery oak, and crisp acidity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Wolf Blass Red Label Chardonnay from Barossa Valley delivers a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Ripe peach and apricot dominate the nose, complemented by citrus zest and subtle floral notes. On the palate, creamy butter and toasted oak provide structure and richness, balanced by medium-high acidity that keeps the wine fresh and lively. A whisper of minerality and hazelnut adds complexity on the finish. This is a versatile, food-friendly white suited to both casual dining and more refined occasions.

name: Dr Loosen  Red Slate Riesling Dry
short_description: Dry Mosel Riesling with slate minerality, citrus zest, and green apple—crisp, food-friendly, and elegant.
description: Dr Loosen's Red Slate Riesling Dry is a single-varietal expression from Germany's Mosel region, crafted to showcase the mineral-driven character of the vineyard's distinctive slate terroir. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by stone-fruit notes of peach and a pronounced minerality that defines the palate. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, dry finish with herbaceous undertones and a lingering slate-driven complexity. This is a versatile food wine suited to seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations.

name: Chateau Musar  White 2010
short_description: Elegant Lebanese white blend of Obaideh & Merwah with citrus, stone fruit & mineral complexity. Aged 2010 vintage.
description: Château Musar's 2010 White represents a distinctive expression of Lebanon's Bekaa Valley terroir, blending 60% Obaideh with 40% Merwah—indigenous varieties that anchor the estate's winemaking philosophy. The wine displays a medium body with bright, medium-high acidity, offering layers of citrus zest and stone fruit on the nose, supported by subtle honey and herbaceous notes. A decade of bottle age has introduced mineral complexity and dried-fruit undertones, creating a wine of both freshness and depth. The balance between primary fruit character and tertiary evolution makes this a versatile food companion.

name: Altano  Vinho Branco Douro DOC
short_description: Douro white blend of Malvasia Fina, Viosinho, Rabigato & Moscatel Galego—crisp, mineral-driven, with stone fruit and floral complexity.
description: Altano Vinho's Branco Douro is a carefully balanced white blend from Portugal's prestigious Douro region, uniting four complementary varieties: Malvasia Fina, Viosinho, Rabigato, and Moscatel Galego. The wine displays a refreshing medium body with bright acidity, opening with stone fruit and citrus aromatics layered with subtle floral and herbaceous notes. A mineral backbone and delicate honey undertones add complexity without heaviness, making this a versatile aperitif or food wine suited to seafood, light pasta, and aromatic Asian cuisine.

name: Max Ferd. Richter  Estate Riesling
short_description: Mosel Riesling with bright peach, apricot, and mineral slate. High acidity, off-dry profile—versatile for seafood and spiced Asian cuisine.
description: Max Ferd. Richter's Estate Riesling from the Mosel showcases the classic hallmarks of German Riesling: a light, elegant frame with vibrant acidity and mineral precision. The nose reveals ripe peach and apricot, complemented by lime and citrus zest, with subtle floral and honeyed undertones. On the palate, the wine's high acidity and off-dry character create a refreshing, food-friendly profile that bridges delicate seafood, spiced Asian cuisine, and fresh appetizers. The slate mineral backbone provides complexity and length, making this a versatile choice for both casual sipping and thoughtful pairing.

name: Voyager Estate  Sauvignon Blanc Semillon
short_description: Margaret River Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon blend: crisp citrus, herbaceous minerality, and stone fruit complexity.
description: Voyager Estate's Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon from Margaret River delivers a refreshing white wine built on vibrant citrus zest and herbaceous character. The 60% Sauvignon Blanc dominates the primary profile with green apple and lime notes, while 40% Semillon adds stone fruit depth and mineral complexity. Medium body and brisk acidity make this an ideal aperitif or food wine, particularly suited to seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations.

name: Annalina  Marlborough Pinot Gris
short_description: Crisp Marlborough Pinot Grigio with vibrant citrus and green apple; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description: Annalina's Marlborough Pinot Grigio delivers the signature brightness and mineral precision of New Zealand's premier white wine region. Dominant citrus zest and green apple notes define the primary character, supported by subtle stone fruit and a clean, refreshing finish. Medium body and medium-high acidity make this wine versatile at table—equally at home as an aperitif or paired with delicate seafood, fresh salads, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Annalina  Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant grapefruit, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description: Annalina's Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc delivers the signature brightness and precision of New Zealand's premier white wine region. Grapefruit and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones that speak to the cool-climate terroir. On the palate, high acidity and mineral texture create a clean, refreshing profile ideal for seafood, fresh cheese, and spice-forward Asian cuisine.

name: Babich Family  Reserve Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description: Babich Family's Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Light-bodied with a clean, refreshing palate, this wine delivers grapefruit and lime on the nose, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones. The high acidity and mineral backbone make it an ideal aperitif and a versatile food companion for seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Duckhorn  Napa Valley Chardonnay
short_description: Full-bodied Napa Chardonnay with ripe apple, vanilla oak, and tropical fruit—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Duckhorn's 2022 Napa Valley Chardonnay exemplifies the region's signature style: a full-bodied white wine with medium acidity and a seamless integration of fruit and oak. Ripe apple and tropical fruit dominate the primary character, while vanilla oak and butter from winemaking add richness and texture. Subtle hazelnut and brioche notes emerge on the finish, lending complexity and elegance. This is a versatile food wine suited to cream-based dishes, shellfish, and roasted poultry.

name: Fraser Gallop  Margaret River Parterre Chardonnay
short_description: Margaret River Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and toasted oak complexity.
description: Fraser Gallop's Parterre Chardonnay from Margaret River showcases the region's signature style: a medium-full bodied white with vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, evolving to ripe stone fruit and subtle minerality on the palate. Oak aging contributes toasted hazelnut and buttery richness without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity and freshness. A versatile food wine suited to both seafood and poultry preparations.

name: Serra Ferdinandea  Bianco IGT
short_description: Sicilian white blend of Grillo and Sauvignon Blanc—crisp citrus, green apple, and mineral notes with refreshing acidity.
description: Serra Ferdinandea Bianco IGT is a Sicilian white wine that marries the indigenous Grillo grape with Sauvignon Blanc in equal measure. The blend showcases bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and tropical undertones that reflect Sicily's sun-drenched terroir. A herbaceous minerality runs through the palate, underpinned by medium-high acidity that delivers a clean, refreshing finish with subtle saline notes. This is an approachable, food-friendly white suited to seafood, light pasta, and warm-weather entertaining.

name: Wither Hills  Chardonnay
short_description: Marlborough Chardonnay with bright apple and citrus, balanced oak, and mineral complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Wither Hills Chardonnay from Marlborough showcases the region's signature precision and cool-climate character. Bright green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, complemented by subtle stone fruit and a whisper of minerality. On the palate, vanilla oak and buttery richness provide structure and depth without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. The finish is clean and lingering, with hazelnut and mineral notes adding complexity. A versatile, well-balanced Chardonnay suited to both casual enjoyment and food pairing.

name: Spy Valley  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant grapefruit, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description: Spy Valley's Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough delivers the region's signature brightness and precision. Light-bodied with piercing acidity, the wine opens with grapefruit and citrus zest, followed by herbaceous and grassy undertones that speak to the cool-climate terroir. Mineral notes and green apple provide structure and freshness on the palate, with a clean, dry finish ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and vibrant Asian cuisine.

name: Dog Point  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus, tropical fruit, and herbaceous minerality.
description: Dog Point's 2024 Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Grapefruit and lime dominate the nose, supported by tropical and herbaceous notes that evolve on the palate. The wine's lean, mineral-driven structure and crisp finish make it an ideal aperitif and versatile food companion.

name: Dog Point  Chardonnay
short_description: Marlborough Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived richness. Elegant and food-friendly.
description: Dog Point's 2021 Chardonnay from Marlborough showcases the region's signature balance of fruit intensity and mineral precision. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit and subtle floral notes. On the palate, oak-derived butter and vanilla add textural richness without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. A touch of toast and minerality on the finish provides complexity and aging potential. This is a classically styled New Zealand Chardonnay—versatile at table and rewarding on its own.

name: Schieferkopf  Pfalz Gewurztraminer Trocken Sec
short_description: Aromatic Pfalz Gewürztraminer with lychee, rose, and spice—dry, refreshing, and food-friendly.
description: A classic dry Gewürztraminer from Germany's Pfalz region, this 2021 vintage showcases the varietal's signature aromatic intensity. Dominant lychee and rose blossom notes on the nose give way to stone fruit and citrus zest on the palate, with supporting honey and white spice adding complexity. Medium body and crisp acidity make it versatile at table—equally at home as an apéritif or paired with Asian cuisine, soft cheeses, and delicate seafood preparations.

name: Weingut Robert Weil  Riesling Rheingau Tradition
short_description: Elegant Rheingau Riesling with bright stone fruit, citrus zest, and mineral precision. High acidity and delicate florals define this classic German white.
description:
Weingut Robert Weil's Riesling Rheingau Tradition exemplifies the precision and elegance of Germany's premier white wine region. This light-bodied expression showcases the hallmark characteristics of Rheingau Riesling: vibrant stone fruit and citrus zest on the nose, supported by pronounced minerality and subtle floral notes. The wine's high acidity provides a crisp, refreshing backbone that carries through to a clean, mineral-driven finish with hints of petrol complexity.

Ideal as an aperitif or paired with delicate seafood, fresh salads, and spicy Asian cuisine where its acidity and brightness cut through bold flavors with grace.

name: Nollen Erben	 Mosel Riesling Spatlese
short_description: Mosel Riesling Spätlese: stone fruit, citrus, and mineral slate with vibrant acidity—elegant and food-friendly.
description: Nollen Erben's Mosel Riesling Spätlese exemplifies the classic profile of Germany's premier white wine region. Light-bodied with piercing acidity, the wine opens with ripe peach and apricot aromas, followed by lime and citrus zest on the palate. A distinctive slate mineral character—hallmark of Mosel terroir—provides structure and complexity. The off-dry character balances the wine's vibrant acidity, making it versatile at table and equally compelling as an aperitif.

name: Nollen Erben	 Mosel Dry Riesling
short_description: Mosel Riesling with vibrant peach, apricot, and mineral slate; crisp acidity and dry profile.
description: Nollen Erben's Mosel Dry Riesling exemplifies the region's signature style: a light-bodied white with piercing acidity and mineral precision. The nose opens with ripe stone fruit—peach and apricot—layered with lime and green apple, while the palate reveals slate minerality and citrus zest. Bone-dry in finish, this Riesling balances fruit-forward charm with structural elegance, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for delicate seafood and spice-forward cuisines.

name: Nollen Erben	 Rheinhessen Gewürztraminer Kabinett
short_description: Aromatic Kabinett from Rheinhessen: floral, lychee-forward Gewürztraminer with refreshing acidity and off-dry balance.
description: Nollen Erben's Rheinhessen Gewürztraminer Kabinett exemplifies the classic German off-dry white style. Gewürztraminer's signature aromatics—lychee, rose, and honeyed stone fruit—dominate the nose, supported by bright citrus zest and subtle spice. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver refreshment without heaviness, while residual sugar (typical of Kabinett classification) adds roundness and approachability. Ideal as an apéritif or alongside aromatic Asian cuisine.

name: Ulrich Langguth  Riseling QBA Dry Traben Gaispfad
short_description: Mosel Riesling with stone fruit purity, mineral slate, and crisp acidity—elegant aperitif or spicy cuisine partner.
description: Ulrich Langguth's Riesling QBA from the Traben Gaispfad vineyard in Germany's Mosel region exemplifies the classic profile of this noble varietal: a light-bodied white with vibrant acidity and mineral-driven complexity. The wine opens with ripe peach and apricot on the nose, supported by citrus zest and a distinctive slate minerality characteristic of the region's schist soils. On the palate, the high acidity provides freshness and structure, while subtle off-dry character adds roundness without heaviness. This is a versatile food wine suited to spicy Asian cuisine, delicate seafood, and casual aperitif service.

name: Sileni Estates  Cellar Selection Pinot Gris Hawke's Bay
short_description: Crisp Pinot Grigio from Hawke's Bay with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description: Sileni Estates' Cellar Selection Pinot Grigio showcases the bright, mineral-driven character typical of New Zealand's Hawke's Bay region. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, followed by delicate stone fruit and pear on the palate. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for fresh seafood, light salads, and Asian cuisine. A versatile, food-friendly white that balances freshness with subtle complexity.

name: Sileni Estates	 The Straits Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc
short_description: Crisp Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, tropical fruit, and herbaceous minerality.
description: Sileni Estates' The Straits Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc delivers the signature brightness and aromatic intensity of New Zealand's premier white wine region. Grapefruit and green apple dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones that speak to the cool-climate terroir. On the palate, tropical fruit notes emerge alongside zesty citrus zest and mineral tension, creating a refreshing, food-friendly profile ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and vibrant Asian cuisine.

name: Opawa  Sauvignon Blanc Marlborough
short_description: Vibrant Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with citrus drive, herbaceous snap, and mineral precision.
description: A classic expression of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc, this wine showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Grapefruit and green apple dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones that speak to the cool-climate terroir. On the palate, tropical notes emerge alongside crisp citrus zest and a mineral backbone, creating a wine of excellent freshness and food-friendly versatility. The light body and high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, salads, and Asian cuisine.

name: Dr Loosen  Dr. L Riesling Dry
short_description: Crisp Mosel Riesling with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Dry, refreshing, food-friendly.
description: Dr. L Riesling Dry from the Mosel region showcases the classic elegance of German Riesling in a bone-dry expression. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, followed by subtle stone fruit and floral aromatics. On the palate, medium body and bracing acidity deliver a clean, mineral-driven finish with hints of lime and herbaceous character. This is a versatile, food-friendly white that bridges casual aperitif service and refined table pairings.

name: Heim  Gewurztraminer Imperial
short_description: Aromatic Alsatian Gewurztraminer with lychee, rose, and white pepper—refreshing and food-friendly.
description: Heim's Gewurztraminer Imperial is a classic expression of Alsace's signature aromatic white. The wine opens with pronounced lychee and rose aromatics, complemented by white pepper spice and floral notes that define the varietal's primary character. Stone fruit and honey emerge on the palate, adding textural richness and subtle sweetness. Medium body and brisk acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly profile suited to spiced cuisines and soft cheeses.

name: Spy Valley  Envoy Sauvignon Blanc
description: Spy Valley  Envoy Sauvignon Blanc

name: I Feudi Di Romans  Pinot Grigio
short_description: Crisp Friuli Pinot Grigio with citrus, green apple, and mineral freshness—ideal aperitif and seafood companion.
description: A classic expression from Friuli-Venezia Giulia, this Pinot Grigio delivers the region's signature clarity and precision. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, with subtle stone fruit and pear notes emerging on the palate. The wine's medium body and vibrant acidity create a refreshing, mineral-driven finish that lingers cleanly. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with light seafood, fresh salads, and delicate preparations that benefit from its crisp, food-friendly profile.

name: Corryton Burge  Eden Valley Riesling
short_description: Crisp Eden Valley Riesling with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision. High acidity and elegant structure.
description: Corryton Burge's Eden Valley Riesling exemplifies the region's cool-climate signature: a light-bodied white wine built on vibrant acidity and mineral-driven complexity. Stone fruit and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by subtle floral notes and a distinctive wet-stone minerality. The palate is clean and refreshing, with green apple and lime acidity that frames peach and citrus flavors. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with delicate seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations.

The botanical profile emphasizes herbaceous and floral notes, delivering a crisp, clean finish typical of contemporary craft gin production. Light-bodied and refreshing, it serves as a versatile aperitif and cocktail base, equally suited to classic gin and tonic or contemporary mixed drinks.

Remy Martin  X.O Excellence (700 ml)
short_description: Fine Champagne XO blending up to 400 eaux-de-vie aged 10–30+ years, distilled on the lees in Limousin oak from chalky Grande & Petite Champagne soils.
description:
Rémy Martin's founding commitment — maintained since 1724 — to source exclusively from Grande Champagne and Petite Champagne, the two highest-ranked Cognac crus, is the structural fact behind every bottle of XO Excellence. No other major house applies this restriction across its entire range; rivals blend from all six crus, reserving Fine Champagne status for prestige tiers only.

The deep chalky-calcareous subsoil of these two crus is the engine of the house style: permeable limestone retains moisture through dry seasons, sustaining grape acidity and concentrating aromatic compounds in the Ugni Blanc fruit that would otherwise flatten in heavier soils.

The XO is assembled from up to 400 individually selected eaux-de-vie, the youngest aged a minimum of 10 years, the oldest extending beyond 30. A defining house signature: distillation on the lees — the residual yeast sediment retained in the still — a practice most Cognac houses abandon before distillation but which Rémy Martin applies house-wide, adding measurable aromatic depth at the source. Maturation proceeds exclusively in French Limousin oak from trees at least 100 years old, seasoned outdoors for 2–3 years; the wide grain of Limousin oak drives pronounced vanilla and spice exchange across the long aging arc.

The XO's special distinction within the range is its span: a single blend bridging a full 20-year maturation window, creating textural density and dried-fruit concentration that the VSOP's shorter aging cannot reach, bottled at 40% ABV.

short_description: Osborne Carlos 1: A full-bodied Spanish brandy with dried fruit, vanilla oak, and warm spice—elegant aperitif or dessert companion.
description: Osborne Carlos 1 is a distinguished Spanish brandy from Jerez, crafted in the solera tradition. The spirit opens with concentrated dried fruit and dark fruit character, layered with rich vanilla oak and warming spice notes. The palate is smooth and full-bodied, with caramel sweetness and subtle tobacco undertones that emerge on the finish. A versatile spirit suited to sipping neat, pairing with dark chocolate and creamy desserts, or enjoying as a sophisticated aperitif alongside Spanish tapas and aged cheeses.

name: Laird's  80 Proof Blended Applejack (750 ml)
short_description: Classic American apple brandy with caramel sweetness, vanilla oak, and warming spice—a versatile spirit for dessert or sipping.
description: Laird's 80 Proof Blended Applejack represents a cornerstone of American brandy tradition, blending apple distillate with neutral grain spirits to create a balanced, approachable spirit. The nose opens with bright apple and caramel notes, followed by vanilla oak and subtle toasted grain. On the palate, medium-full body carries flavors of dried fruit, warm spice, and a gentle smoke undertone, finishing with lingering sweetness and oak warmth. The 80-proof bottling strikes an equilibrium between intensity and drinkability, making it suitable for neat sipping, cocktails, or pairing with desserts and rich comfort fare.
